title: ResearchEVO: An End-to-End Framework for Automated Scientific Discovery and Documentation
authors: Zhe Zhao, Haibin Wen, Jiaming Ma, Jiachang Zhan, Tianyi Xu, Ye Wei, Qingfu Zhang
abstract: An important recurring pattern in scientific breakthroughs is a two-stage process: an initial phase of undirected experimentation that yields an unexpected finding, followed by a retrospective phase that explains why the finding works and situates it within existing theory. We present ResearchEVO, an end-to-end framework that computationally instantiates this discover-then-explain paradigm. The Evolution Phase employs LLM-guided bi-dimensional co-evolution -- simultaneously optimizing both algorithmic logic and overall architecture -- to search the space of code implementations purely by fitness, without requiring any understanding of the solutions it produces. The Writing Phase then takes the best-performing algorithm and autonomously generates a complete, publication-ready research paper through sentence-level retrieval-augmented generation with explicit anti-hallucination verification and automated experiment design. To our knowledge, ResearchEVO is the first system to cover this full pipeline end to end: no prior work jointly performs principled algorithm evolution and literature-grounded scientific documentation. We validate the framework on two cross-disciplinary scientific problems -- Quantum Error Correction using real Google quantum hardware data, and Physics-Informed Neural Networks -- where the Evolution Phase discovered human-interpretable algorithmic mechanisms that had not been previously proposed in the respective domain literatures. In both cases, the Writing Phase autonomously produced compilable LaTeX manuscripts that correctly grounded these blind discoveries in existing theory via RAG, with zero fabricated citations.
title_zh: ResearchEVO：一种用于自动化科学发现与文档记录的端到端框架
abstract_zh: 科学突破中一个重要的反复出现的模式是两阶段过程：首先是无定向实验的初始阶段，产生意外发现；随后是回溯性阶段，解释该发现为何有效，并将其置于现有理论框架之中。我们提出了 ResearchEVO，一个计算上实现这一“先发现、后解释”范式的端到端框架。在进化阶段（Evolution Phase），系统采用由大语言模型（LLM）引导的二维协同进化机制——同时优化算法逻辑与整体架构——仅通过适应度评估来搜索代码实现空间，无需理解所生成解决方案的具体含义。在写作阶段（Writing Phase），系统则自动选取表现最佳的算法，通过基于句子级别的检索增强生成（RAG），结合显式的反幻觉验证和自动化实验设计，自动生成完整且可直接投稿的研究论文。据我们所知，ResearchEVO 是首个实现这一全流程端到端覆盖的系统：此前没有任何工作能同时完成有原则的算法演化与基于文献的科学文档撰写。我们在两个跨学科科学问题上验证了该框架的有效性：一是利用真实谷歌量子硬件数据进行量子纠错，二是物理信息神经网络。在这两个案例中，进化阶段均发现了人类可理解的算法机制，这些机制此前在相关领域文献中从未被提出。在每种情况下，写作阶段都自主生成了可编译的 LaTeX 论文手稿，准确地通过 RAG 将这些“盲发现”纳入现有理论体系，且未产生任何虚构引用。

title: OmniDiagram: Advancing Unified Diagram Code Generation via Visual Interrogation Reward
authors: Haoyue Yang, Xuanle Zhao, Xuexin Liu, Feibang Jiang, Yao Zhu
abstract: The paradigm of programmable diagram generation is evolving rapidly, playing a crucial role in structured visualization. However, most existing studies are confined to a narrow range of task formulations and language support, constraining their applicability to diverse diagram types. In this work, we propose OmniDiagram, a unified framework that incorporates diverse diagram code languages and task definitions. To address the challenge of aligning code logic with visual fidelity in Reinforcement Learning (RL), we introduce a novel visual feedback strategy named Visual Interrogation Verifies All (\textsc{Viva}). Unlike brittle syntax-based rules or pixel-level matching, \textsc{Viva} rewards the visual structure of rendered diagrams through a generative approach. Specifically, \textsc{Viva} actively generates targeted visual inquiries to scrutinize diagram visual fidelity and provides fine-grained feedback for optimization. This mechanism facilitates a self-evolving training process, effectively obviating the need for manually annotated ground truth code. Furthermore, we construct M3$^2$Diagram, the first large-scale diagram code generation dataset, containing over 196k high-quality instances. Experimental results confirm that the combination of SFT and our \textsc{Viva}-based RL allows OmniDiagram to establish a new state-of-the-art (SOTA) across diagram code generation benchmarks.
title_zh: OmniDiagram：通过视觉询问奖励推进统一图表代码生成
abstract_zh: 可编程图表生成的范式正在迅速演进，在结构化可视化中发挥着至关重要的作用。然而，现有大多数研究局限于狭窄的任务形式和语言支持范围，限制了其在多样化图表类型中的适用性。本文提出 OmniDiagram，一个统一的框架，能够整合多种图表代码语言与任务定义。为解决强化学习（RL）中代码逻辑与视觉保真度对齐的挑战，我们引入一种新颖的视觉反馈策略——视觉质询全验证（Visual Interrogation Verifies All, \textsc{Viva}）。与脆弱的语法规则或像素级匹配不同，\textsc{Viva} 通过生成式方法奖励渲染后图表的视觉结构。具体而言，\textsc{Viva} 主动生成针对性的视觉问题，以细致审查图表的视觉保真度，并提供细粒度的反馈用于优化。该机制促进了自我演化的训练过程，有效避免了对人工标注真实代码的需求。此外，我们构建了 M3$^2$Diagram，这是首个大规模的图表代码生成数据集，包含超过19.6万条高质量样本。实验结果表明，结合监督微调（SFT）与基于 \textsc{Viva} 的强化学习，OmniDiagram 在各类图表代码生成基准上均达到了新的最先进水平（SOTA）。

title: Your LLM Agent Can Leak Your Data: Data Exfiltration via Backdoored Tool Use
authors: Wuyang Zhang, Shichao Pei
abstract: Tool-use large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly deployed to support sensitive workflows, relying on tool calls for retrieval, external API access, and session memory management. While prior research has examined various threats, the risk of systematic data exfiltration by backdoored agents remains underexplored. In this work, we present Back-Reveal, a data exfiltration attack that embeds semantic triggers into fine-tuned LLM agents. When triggered, the backdoored agent invokes memory-access tool calls to retrieve stored user context and exfiltrates it via disguised retrieval tool calls. We further demonstrate that multi-turn interaction amplifies the impact of data exfiltration, as attacker-controlled retrieval responses can subtly steer subsequent agent behavior and user interactions, enabling sustained and cumulative information leakage over time. Our experimental results expose a critical vulnerability in LLM agents with tool access and highlight the need for defenses against exfiltration-oriented backdoors.
title_zh: 你的LLM代理可能会泄露你的数据：通过被植入后门的工具使用导致的数据外泄
abstract_zh: 工具使用的大语言模型（LLM）代理正被越来越多地部署于敏感工作流程中，依赖工具调用实现信息检索、外部API访问以及会话记忆管理。尽管先前的研究已探讨了多种威胁，但关于后门代理系统性数据窃取的风险仍鲜有深入研究。本文提出一种名为Back-Reveal的数据外泄攻击方法，该方法通过微调将语义触发器嵌入LLM代理中。当触发条件满足时，被植入后门的代理会调用内存访问工具，获取存储的用户上下文信息，并通过伪装成正常检索操作的工具调用将数据外泄出去。我们进一步证明，多轮交互显著放大了数据外泄的影响：攻击者可控制的检索响应能够微妙地引导后续代理行为及用户互动，从而在时间上持续且累积地造成信息泄露。实验结果揭示了具备工具访问权限的LLM代理存在关键安全漏洞，凸显了防范以数据外泄为目标的后门攻击的迫切需求。

title: Multi-Drafter Speculative Decoding with Alignment Feedback
authors: Taehyeon Kim, Hojung Jung, Se-Young Yun
abstract: Speculative decoding (SD) accelerates large language model (LLM) inference by using a smaller model to draft future tokens, which are then verified by the target LLM. This preserves generation quality by accepting only aligned tokens. However, individual drafters, often trained for specific tasks or domains, exhibit limited effectiveness across diverse applications. To address this, we introduce \textsc{MetaSD}, a unified framework that integrates multiple drafters into the SD process. MetaSD dynamically allocates computational resources to heterogeneous drafters by leveraging alignment feedback and framing drafter selection as a multi-armed bandit problem. Extensive experiments show MetaSD consistently outperforms single-drafter approaches.
title_zh: 多起草者推测解码与对齐反馈
abstract_zh: 推测解码（Speculative Decoding, SD）通过使用一个较小的模型来预生成未来的词元，再由目标大语言模型（LLM）进行验证，从而加速大语言模型的推理过程。该方法通过仅接受对齐的词元来保持生成质量。然而，单独使用的预生成器（drafter）通常针对特定任务或领域进行训练，在多样化应用场景中表现有限。为解决这一问题，我们提出 \textsc{MetaSD}，一个将多个预生成器统一整合到SD流程中的框架。MetaSD通过利用对齐反馈动态分配计算资源，并将预生成器选择建模为多臂赌博机（multi-armed bandit）问题，实现对异构预生成器的有效调度。大量实验表明，MetaSD在各项指标上均持续优于单一预生成器的方法。

title: Broken by Default: A Formal Verification Study of Security Vulnerabilities in AI-Generated Code
authors: Dominik Blain, Maxime Noiseux
abstract: AI coding assistants are now used to generate production code in security-sensitive domains, yet the exploitability of their outputs remains unquantified. We address this gap with Broken by Default: a formal verification study of 3,500 code artifacts generated by seven frontier LLMs across 500 security-critical prompts (five CWE categories, 100 prompts each). Each artifact is subjected to the Z3 SMT solver via the COBALT analysis pipeline, producing mathematical satisfiability witnesses rather than pattern-based heuristics. Across all models, 55.8% of artifacts contain at least one COBALT-identified vulnerability; of these, 1,055 are formally proven via Z3 satisfiability witnesses. GPT-4o leads at 62.4% (grade F); Gemini 2.5 Flash performs best at 48.4% (grade D). No model achieves a grade better than D. Six of seven representative findings are confirmed with runtime crashes under GCC AddressSanitizer. Three auxiliary experiments show: (1) explicit security instructions reduce the mean rate by only 4 points; (2) six industry tools combined miss 97.8% of Z3-proven findings; and (3) models identify their own vulnerable outputs 78.7% of the time in review mode yet generate them at 55.8% by default.
title_zh: 默认即已损坏：对AI生成代码中安全漏洞的形式化验证研究
abstract_zh: AI编程助手如今已被用于生成安全敏感领域的生产代码，但其输出结果的可利用性尚未得到量化评估。针对这一空白，我们提出了“默认易损”（Broken by Default）研究：一项形式化验证分析，涵盖由七款前沿大语言模型在500个安全关键提示下生成的3,500个代码样本（涉及五个CWE类别，每类100个提示）。每个代码样本均通过COBALT分析流程交由Z3 SMT求解器进行检测，生成的是数学可满足性证明，而非基于模式的启发式判断。结果显示，所有模型生成的代码中，55.8%至少包含一个COBALT识别出的漏洞；其中1,055个漏洞经Z3可满足性证明被正式确认。GPT-4o表现最差，漏洞率为62.4%（评级F），而Gemini 2.5 Flash表现最佳，漏洞率为48.4%（评级D）。没有任何模型的评级优于D。六项代表性发现经GCC AddressSanitizer运行时检测后均证实会导致崩溃。三项辅助实验进一步揭示：（1）明确的安全指令仅使平均漏洞率降低4个百分点；（2）六款工业级工具联合使用仍遗漏97.8%的Z3已证明的漏洞；（3）在审查模式下，模型能识别自身输出中的漏洞达78.7%，但在默认生成状态下仍以55.8%的概率产生漏洞。

title: Reinforcement Learning with Negative Tests as Completeness Signal for Formal Specification Synthesis
authors: Zhechong Huang, Zhao Zhang, Zeyu Sun, Huifeng Sun, Yingfei Xiong
abstract: The specification synthesis task aims to automatically generate specifications, together with any necessary auxiliary verification annotations, for existing programs. This task is important because such specifications serve as behavioral contracts that support modular reasoning and reusable verification across a codebase. At the same time, it remains challenging because verifier-only feedback is fundamentally incomplete: passing verification establishes soundness, but cannot distinguish weak specifications from strong ones. What is missing is a fine-grained signal for specification completeness. We present SpecRL, a reinforcement learning framework for specification synthesis in Dafny. SpecRL introduces a self-contained pipeline that generates negative tests, i.e., input-output pairs that can never be produced by the program. We use the fraction of these negative tests rejected by a candidate specification as a signal of specification completeness, which is integrated into the reward for RL training. Experiments across four model sizes show that SpecRL improves both specification strength and verification success over SFT and RL with a binary specification-strength reward, generalizes to an out-of-distribution benchmark, and remains competitive on that unseen benchmark compared to much larger general-purpose LLMs.
title_zh: 基于负例测试作为完备性信号的强化学习在形式规范合成中的应用
abstract_zh: 规范合成任务旨在为现有程序自动生成规范，以及任何必要的辅助验证注释。这一任务至关重要，因为此类规范作为行为契约，支持模块化推理和代码库中的可复用验证。然而，该任务仍具挑战性，因为仅依赖验证器的反馈本质上是不完整的：通过验证只能保证正确性，却无法区分弱规范与强规范之间的差异。真正缺失的是对规范完备性的细粒度信号。我们提出了SpecRL，一种用于Dafny语言的强化学习规范合成框架。SpecRL引入了一个自包含的流程，能够生成负向测试用例，即程序永远无法产生的输入-输出对。我们利用候选规范拒绝这些负向测试用例的比例，作为规范完备性的信号，并将其融入强化学习的奖励机制中。在四个不同规模模型上的实验表明，SpecRL在规范强度和验证成功率方面均优于基于监督微调（SFT）及采用二元规范强度奖励的强化学习方法；它还能泛化到分布外的基准测试，并在未见过的基准上表现优异，甚至与远大于它的通用大语言模型（LLM）相媲美。

title: What Models Know, How Well They Know It: Knowledge-Weighted Fine-Tuning for Learning When to Say "I Don't Know"
authors: Joosung Lee, Hwiyeol Jo, Donghyeon Ko, Kyubyung Chae, Cheonbok Park, Jeonghoon Kim
abstract: While large language models (LLMs) demonstrate strong capabilities across diverse user queries, they still suffer from hallucinations, often arising from knowledge misalignment between pre-training and fine-tuning. To address this misalignment, we reliably estimate a fine-grained, instance-level knowledge score via multi-sampled inference. Using the knowledge score, we scale the learning signal according to the model's existing knowledge, while encouraging explicit "I don't know" responses for out-of-scope queries. Experimental results show that this approach allows the model to explicitly express uncertainty when it lacks knowledge, while maintaining accuracy on questions it can answer. Furthermore, we propose evaluation metrics for uncertainty, showing that accurate discrimination between known and unknown instances consistently improves performance.
title_zh: 模型知道什么，以及它们了解的程度：基于知识加权的微调以学习何时说“我不知道”
abstract_zh: 尽管大型语言模型（LLMs）在应对各种用户查询时展现出强大的能力，但仍存在幻觉问题，这通常源于预训练与微调阶段之间的知识不一致。为解决这一知识错位问题，我们通过多轮采样推理可靠地估计细粒度的、实例级别的知识得分。基于该知识得分，我们根据模型现有的知识水平动态调整学习信号，并鼓励模型对超出范围的问题明确作出“我不知道”的回应。实验结果表明，该方法使模型在缺乏知识时能够明确表达不确定性，同时在可回答的问题上保持高准确性。此外，我们提出了用于评估不确定性的新指标，结果显示，准确区分已知与未知实例能持续提升模型性能。

title: FinReporting: An Agentic Workflow for Localized Reporting of Cross-Jurisdiction Financial Disclosures
authors: Fan Zhang, Mingzi Song, Rania Elbadry, Yankai Chen, Shaobo Wang, Yixi Zhou, Xunwen Zheng, Yueru He, Yuyang Dai, Georgi Georgiev, Ayesha Gull, Muhammad Usman Safder, Fan Wu, Liyuan Meng, Fengxian Ji, Junning Zhao, Xueqing Peng, Jimin Huang, Yu Chen, Xue, Liu, Preslav Nakov, Zhuohan Xie
abstract: Financial reporting systems increasingly use large language models (LLMs) to extract and summarize corporate disclosures. However, most assume a single-market setting and do not address structural differences across jurisdictions. Variations in accounting taxonomies, tagging infrastructures (e.g., XBRL vs. PDF), and aggregation conventions make cross-jurisdiction reporting a semantic alignment and verification challenge. We present FinReporting, an agentic workflow for localized cross-jurisdiction financial reporting. The system builds a unified canonical ontology over Income Statement, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow, and decomposes reporting into auditable stages including filing acquisition, extraction, canonical mapping, and anomaly logging. Rather than using LLMs as free-form generators, FinReporting deploys them as constrained verifiers under explicit decision rules and evidence grounding. Evaluated on annual filings from the US, Japan, and China, the system improves consistency and reliability under heterogeneous reporting regimes. We release an interactive demo supporting cross-market inspection and structured export of localized financial statements. Our demo is available at https://huggingface.co/spaces/BoomQ/FinReporting-Demo . The video describing our system is available at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f65jdEL31Kk
title_zh: FinReporting：一种用于跨司法管辖区财务披露本地化报告的智能工作流
abstract_zh: 财务报告系统越来越多地利用大型语言模型（LLMs）来提取和总结企业披露信息。然而，大多数现有方法假设处于单一市场环境，未能考虑不同司法管辖区之间的结构性差异。会计分类标准、标记基础设施（如XBRL与PDF）以及汇总惯例的差异，使得跨司法管辖区的财务报告面临语义对齐与验证的挑战。我们提出了FinReporting——一种面向本地化跨司法管辖区财务报告的智能体工作流系统。该系统构建了关于损益表、资产负债表和现金流量表的统一规范本体，并将报告流程分解为可审计的多个阶段，包括文件获取、信息提取、规范映射和异常日志记录。与将LLM用作自由生成工具的传统方式不同，FinReporting将LLM作为在明确决策规则和证据支撑下的受限验证器使用。我们在美国、日本和中国的企业年度报告数据上进行了评估，结果表明该系统在异构报告制度下显著提升了报告的一致性与可靠性。我们已发布一个交互式演示系统，支持跨市场审查及本地化财务报表的结构化导出。演示地址：https://huggingface.co/spaces/BoomQ/FinReporting-Demo。系统介绍视频请访问：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f65jdEL31Kk

title: Does Pass Rate Tell the Whole Story? Evaluating Design Constraint Compliance in LLM-based Issue Resolution
authors: Kai Yu, Zhenhao Zhou, Junhao Zeng, Ying Wang, Xueying Du, Zhiqiang Yuan, Junwei Liu, Ziyu Zhou, Yujia Wang, Chong Wang, Xin Peng
abstract: Repository-level issue resolution benchmarks have become a standard testbed for evaluating LLM-based agents, yet success is still predominantly measured by test pass rates. In practice, however, acceptable patches must also comply with project-specific design constraints, such as architectural conventions, error-handling policies, and maintainability requirements, which are rarely encoded in tests and are often documented only implicitly in code review discussions. This paper introduces \textit{design-aware issue resolution} and presents \bench{}, a benchmark that makes such implicit design constraints explicit and measurable. \bench{} is constructed by mining and validating design constraints from real-world pull requests, linking them to issue instances, and automatically checking patch compliance using an LLM-based verifier, yielding 495 issues and 1,787 validated constraints across six repositories, aligned with SWE-bench-Verified and SWE-bench-Pro. Experiments with state-of-the-art agents show that test-based correctness substantially overestimates patch quality: fewer than half of resolved issues are fully design-satisfying, design violations are widespread, and functional correctness exhibits negligible statistical association with design satisfaction. While providing issue-specific design guidance reduces violations, substantial non-compliance remains, highlighting a fundamental gap in current agent capabilities and motivating design-aware evaluation beyond functional correctness.
title_zh: 通过率能否讲述全部故事？评估基于大语言模型的缺陷修复中设计约束的符合性
abstract_zh: 代码库级别的问题修复基准已成为评估基于大语言模型（LLM）智能体的标准测试平台，然而目前的成功度量仍主要依赖于测试通过率。在实际应用中，可接受的补丁还必须符合项目特定的设计约束，例如架构规范、错误处理策略以及可维护性要求等。这些约束通常未被纳入测试用例中，往往仅以隐式方式记录在代码审查讨论中。本文提出了“设计感知的问题修复”概念，并引入 \bench{}——一个将此类隐式设计约束显式化且可度量的基准。\bench{} 通过挖掘和验证真实世界拉取请求中的设计约束，将其与具体问题实例关联，并利用基于大语言模型的验证器自动检查补丁的合规性，最终在六个代码库中构建了包含495个问题和1,787条经验证约束的数据集，其结构与 SWE-bench-Verified 和 SWE-bench-Pro 保持一致。对前沿智能体的实验表明，仅基于测试正确性的评估会显著高估补丁质量：不足一半的已解决问题具备完全的设计合规性；设计违规现象普遍存在；而功能正确性与设计满足度之间几乎不存在统计相关性。尽管提供针对具体问题的设计指导能够减少违规，但仍有大量不合规情况存在，揭示了当前智能体能力的根本性差距，也凸显出超越功能正确性的、面向设计感知的评估体系的必要性。

title: Foundations for Agentic AI Investigations from the Forensic Analysis of OpenClaw
authors: Jan Gruber, Jan-Niclas Hilgert
abstract: Agentic Al systems are increasingly deployed as personal assistants and are likely to become a common object of digital investigations. However, little is known about how their internal state and actions can be reconstructed during forensic analysis. Despite growing popularity, systematic forensic approaches for such systems remain largely unexplored. This paper presents an empirical study of OpenClaw a widely used single-agent assistant. We examine OpenClaw's technical design via static code analysis and apply differential forensic analysis to identify recoverable traces across stages of the agent interaction loop. We classify and correlate these traces to assess their investigative value in a systematic way. Based on these observations, we propose an agent artifact taxonomy that captures recurring investigative patterns. Finally, we highlight a foundational challenge for agentic Al forensics: agent-mediated execution introduces an additional layer of abstraction and substantial nondeterminism in trace generation. The large language model (LLM), the execution environment, and the evolving context can influence tool choice and state transitions in ways that are largely absent from rule-based software. Overall, our results provide an initial foundation for the systematic investigation of agentic Al and outline implications for digital forensic practice and future research.
title_zh: 基于OpenClaw的取证分析构建代理型人工智能调查的基础
abstract_zh: 代理型人工智能系统正越来越多地被用作个人助手，未来很可能成为数字取证中的常见对象。然而，目前人们对如何在取证分析中重建这类系统的内部状态和行为仍知之甚少。尽管其应用日益普及，但针对此类系统的系统性取证方法仍基本处于未探索状态。本文对一种广泛使用的单代理助手OpenClaw进行了实证研究。我们通过静态代码分析考察了OpenClaw的技术设计，并采用差异取证分析方法，在代理交互循环的各个阶段识别可恢复的痕迹。我们将这些痕迹进行分类与关联，以系统化的方式评估其在调查中的价值。基于上述发现，我们提出了一种代理型AI痕迹分类体系，用以捕捉反复出现的调查模式。最后，我们指出了代理型AI取证面临的一个根本性挑战：代理中介执行引入了额外的抽象层次，并在痕迹生成过程中带来了显著的非确定性。大型语言模型（LLM）、执行环境以及不断演化的上下文，均可能以规则化软件中罕见的方式影响工具选择和状态转换。总体而言，我们的研究结果为系统化开展代理型AI取证奠定了初步基础，并对数字取证实践及未来研究提出了重要启示。

title: ETR: Entropy Trend Reward for Efficient Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
authors: Xuan Xiong, Huan Liu, Li Gu, Zhixiang Chi, Yue Qiu, Yuanhao Yu, Yang Wang
abstract: Chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning improves large language model performance on complex tasks, but often produces excessively long and inefficient reasoning traces. Existing methods shorten CoTs using length penalties or global entropy reduction, implicitly assuming that low uncertainty is desirable throughout reasoning. We show instead that reasoning efficiency is governed by the trajectory of uncertainty. CoTs with dominant downward entropy trends are substantially shorter. Motivated by this insight, we propose Entropy Trend Reward (ETR), a trajectory-aware objective that encourages progressive uncertainty reduction while allowing limited local exploration. We integrate ETR into Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) and evaluate it across multiple reasoning models and challenging benchmarks. ETR consistently achieves a superior accuracy-efficiency tradeoff, improving DeepSeek-R1-Distill-7B by 9.9% in accuracy while reducing CoT length by 67% across four benchmarks. Code is available at https://github.com/Xuan1030/ETR
title_zh: ETR：用于高效思维链推理的熵趋势奖励
abstract_zh: 思维链（Chain-of-thought, CoT）推理能够提升大语言模型在复杂任务上的表现，但通常会产生过长且低效的推理过程。现有方法通过引入长度惩罚或全局熵减来缩短CoT，其隐含假设是整个推理过程中保持低不确定性为理想状态。然而，我们发现推理效率实际上由不确定性的变化轨迹所决定：具有显著下降熵趋势的CoT要短得多。基于这一洞察，我们提出了**熵趋势奖励**（Entropy Trend Reward, ETR），这是一种轨迹感知的目标函数，能够在鼓励逐步降低不确定性的同时，允许有限的局部探索。我们将ETR集成到分组相对策略优化（Group Relative Policy Optimization, GRPO）中，并在多个推理模型和具有挑战性的基准测试上进行了评估。结果表明，ETR始终能实现更优的准确率与效率权衡，在四个基准上使DeepSeek-R1-Distill-7B的准确率提升9.9%，同时将CoT长度减少67%。代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/Xuan1030/ETR

title: Swiss-Bench 003: Evaluating LLM Reliability and Adversarial Security for Swiss Regulatory Contexts
authors: Fatih Uenal
abstract: The deployment of large language models (LLMs) in Swiss financial and regulatory contexts demands empirical evidence of both production reliability and adversarial security, dimensions not jointly operationalized in existing Swiss-focused evaluation frameworks. This paper introduces Swiss-Bench 003 (SBP-003), extending the HAAS (Helvetic AI Assessment Score) from six to eight dimensions by adding D7 (Self-Graded Reliability Proxy) and D8 (Adversarial Security). I evaluate ten frontier models across 808 Swiss-specific items in four languages (German, French, Italian, English), comprising seven Swiss-adapted benchmarks (Swiss TruthfulQA, Swiss IFEval, Swiss SimpleQA, Swiss NIAH, Swiss PII-Scope, System Prompt Leakage, and Swiss German Comprehension) targeting FINMA Guidance 08/2024, the revised Federal Act on Data Protection (nDSG), and OWASP Top 10 for LLMs. Self-graded D7 scores (73-94%) exceed externally judged D8 security scores (20-61%) by a wide margin, though these dimensions use non-comparable scoring regimes. System prompt leakage resistance ranges from 24.8% to 88.2%, while PII extraction defense remains weak (14-42%) across all models. Qwen 3.5 Plus achieves the highest self-graded D7 score (94.4%), while GPT-oss 120B achieves the highest D8 score (60.7%) despite being the lowest-cost model evaluated. All evaluations are zero-shot under provider default settings; D7 is self-graded and does not constitute independently validated accuracy. I provide conceptual mapping tables relating benchmark dimensions to FINMA model validation requirements, nDSG data protection obligations, and OWASP LLM risk categories.
title_zh: 瑞士基准003：评估大语言模型在瑞士监管环境下的可靠性与对抗性安全性
abstract_zh:
在瑞士金融与监管语境下部署大型语言模型（LLMs）需要实证证据证明其生产可靠性与对抗性安全性，而这两个维度在现有的瑞士导向评估框架中并未得到协同考量。本文提出瑞士基准003（SBP-003），将原有的HAAS（瑞士人工智能评估得分）从六个维度扩展至八个维度，新增D7（自评可靠性代理）和D8（对抗性安全性）。我针对十款前沿模型，在涵盖四种语言（德语、法语、意大利语、英语）的808个瑞士特定任务上进行了评估，这些任务基于七个经过瑞士本地化调整的基准测试：瑞士TruthfulQA、瑞士IFEval、瑞士SimpleQA、瑞士NIAH、瑞士PII-Scope、系统提示泄露检测以及瑞士德语理解能力测试，旨在覆盖FINMA 2024年第08号指引、修订后的联邦数据保护法（nDSG）以及面向LLM的OWASP十大风险。

自评D7得分（73%-94%）显著高于外部评判的D8安全得分（20%-61%），尽管两者的评分体系不可直接比较。系统提示泄露防御能力介于24.8%至88.2%之间，而所有模型在个人身份信息（PII）提取防护方面均表现薄弱（14%-42%）。Qwen 3.5 Plus在D7自评中取得最高分（94.4%），而GPT-oss 120B虽为成本最低的模型，却在D8安全得分中表现最佳（60.7%）。所有评估均采用零样本设置，且在各模型提供方默认配置下进行；D7为自评结果，不构成独立验证的准确性指标。本文还提供了概念映射表，将各基准维度与FINMA模型验证要求、nDSG数据保护义务以及OWASP LLM风险类别相对应，以促进政策与技术实践之间的衔接。

title: Hackers or Hallucinators? A Comprehensive Analysis of LLM-Based Automated Penetration Testing
authors: Jiaren Peng, Zeqin Li, Chang You, Yan Wang, Hanlin Sun, Xuan Tian, Shuqiao Zhang, Junyi Liu, Jianguo Zhao, Renyang Liu, Haoran Ou, Yuqiang Sun, Jiancheng Zhang, Yutong Jiao, Kunshu Song, Chao Zhang, Fan Shi, Hongda Sun, Rui Yan, Cheng Huang
abstract: The rapid advancement of Large Language Models (LLMs) has created new opportunities for Automated Penetration Testing (AutoPT), spawning numerous frameworks aimed at achieving end-to-end autonomous attacks. However, despite the proliferation of related studies, existing research generally lacks systematic architectural analysis and large-scale empirical comparisons under a unified benchmark. Therefore, this paper presents the first Systematization of Knowledge (SoK) focusing on the architectural design and comprehensive empirical evaluation of current LLM-based AutoPT frameworks. At systematization level, we comprehensively review existing framework designs across six dimensions: agent architecture, agent plan, agent memory, agent execution, external knowledge, and benchmarks. At empirical level, we conduct large-scale experiments on 13 representative open-source AutoPT frameworks and 2 baseline frameworks utilizing a unified benchmark. The experiments consumed over 10 billion tokens in total and generated more than 1,500 execution logs, which were manually reviewed and analyzed over four months by a panel of more than 15 researchers with expertise in cybersecurity. By investigating the latest progress in this rapidly developing field, we provide researchers with a structured taxonomy to understand existing LLM-based AutoPT frameworks and a large-scale empirical benchmark, along with promising directions for future research.
title_zh: 黑客还是幻觉制造者？基于大语言模型的自动化渗透测试全面分析
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）的快速发展为自动化渗透测试（AutoPT）带来了新的机遇，催生了众多旨在实现端到端自主攻击的框架。然而，尽管相关研究层出不穷，现有工作普遍缺乏在统一基准下的系统性架构分析与大规模实证比较。因此，本文首次提出针对当前基于大语言模型的自动化渗透测试框架的“知识体系化”（Systematization of Knowledge, SoK）研究，重点聚焦于其架构设计与全面的实证评估。在体系化层面，我们从六个维度对现有框架设计进行了全面回顾：智能体架构、智能体规划、智能体记忆、智能体执行、外部知识以及评估基准。在实证层面，我们在一个统一的基准上对13个具有代表性的开源AutoPT框架及2个基线框架进行了大规模实验，总消耗超过100亿个token，并生成了超过1500条执行日志。这些日志由超过15名具备网络安全专业背景的研究人员历时四个月进行人工审查与深入分析。通过对该快速发展的领域最新进展的系统考察，本文为研究人员提供了理解现有基于LLM的AutoPT框架的结构化分类体系，建立了一个大规模实证基准，并指出了未来研究的若干有前景的方向。

title: An Iterative Test-and-Repair Framework for Competitive Code Generation
authors: Lingxiao Tang, Muyang Ye, Zhaoyang Chu, Xiaoxue Ren, Zhongxin Liu, Lingfeng Bao, He Ye
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have made remarkable progress in code generation, but competitive programming remains a challenge. Recent training-based methods have improved code generation by using reinforcement learning (RL) with execution feedback. The more recent framework CURE further incorporates test generation into the training process, jointly training a Coder and a Tester within a single model. At inference time, the Coder generates many candidate programs, and the Tester generates tests from the problem description. The candidate who passes the most of the generated tests is selected as the final answer. However, CURE has two critical limitations. First, the Tester never reads any candidate code, so its tests often fail to expose implementation-specific bugs. Second, the Coder generates every candidate from scratch and never learns to fix a buggy program based on a failed test. To address these limitations, we propose FixAudit, which approaches competitive code generation from a new perspective: starting from a single initial candidate, it iteratively improves the candidate through a targeted test-and-repair debugging cycle. The framework trains one shared model with two specialized roles through four stages: the Fixer, which repairs the current candidate based on a failing test, and the Auditor, which reads the candidate code to generate new tests that expose its remaining bugs. We evaluate FixAudit on three benchmarks: APPS, CodeContests, and xCodeEval. Applied to a 7B model, the framework surpasses the average performance of the larger 32B baseline within the same model family under the zero-shot setting. Compared to strong baselines built on the same 7B base model, FixAudit improves average Pass@1 by 35.1% to 36.8% and average AvgPassRatio by 7.1% to 24.5%.
title_zh: 一种用于竞争性代码生成的迭代式测试与修复框架
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型（LLMs）在代码生成方面取得了显著进展，但竞技编程仍然是一个挑战。近期基于训练的方法通过使用执行反馈的强化学习（RL）提升了代码生成效果。更近的框架CURE进一步将测试用例生成融入训练过程，在单一模型中联合训练“编码器”（Coder）和“测试器”（Tester）。推理时，编码器生成多个候选程序，测试器则根据题目描述生成测试用例，最终选择通过最多生成测试用例的候选程序作为答案。然而，CURE存在两个关键局限：第一，测试器从不读取任何候选代码，因此生成的测试往往无法暴露与具体实现相关的缺陷；第二，编码器每次均从头生成候选程序，从未学会根据失败的测试结果来修复有缺陷的代码。

为解决上述问题，我们提出FixAudit，从全新视角应对竞技编程中的代码生成任务：从一个初始候选程序出发，通过有针对性的“测试-修复”迭代调试循环逐步优化该程序。该框架通过四个阶段训练一个共享模型，赋予其两种专业化角色：修复者（Fixer），根据失败的测试用例修复当前候选程序；审计员（Auditor），阅读候选代码以生成能暴露剩余缺陷的新测试用例。

我们在三个基准数据集（APPS、CodeContests 和 xCodeEval）上对FixAudit进行了评估。在7B规模模型上应用该框架，其在零样本设置下的平均表现超越了同模型家族中更大的32B基线模型。相较于基于相同7B基础模型构建的强基线方法，FixAudit将平均Pass@1提升35.1%至36.8%，平均AvgPassRatio提升7.1%至24.5%。

title: On the Role of Fault Localization Context for LLM-Based Program Repair
authors: Melika Sepidband, Hung Viet Pham, Hadi Hemmati
abstract: Fault Localization (FL) is a key component of Large Language Model (LLM)-based Automated Program Repair (APR), yet its impact remains underexplored. In particular, it is unclear how much localization is needed, whether additional context beyond the predicted buggy location is beneficial, and how such context should be retrieved. We conduct a large-scale empirical study on 500 SWE-bench Verified instances using GPT-5-mini, evaluating 61 configurations that vary file-level, element-level, and line-level context. Our results show that more context does not consistently improve repair performance. File-level localization is the dominant factor, yielding a 15-17x improvement over a no-file baseline. Expanding file context is often associated with improved performance, with successful repairs most commonly observed in configurations with approximately 6-10 relevant files. Element-level context expansion provides conditional gains that depend strongly on the file context quality, while line-level context expansion frequently degrades performance due to noise amplification. LLM-based retrieval generally outperforms structural heuristics while using fewer files and tokens. Overall, the most effective FL context strategy typically combines a broad semantic understanding at higher abstraction levels with precise line-level localization. These findings challenge our assumption that increasing the localization context uniformly improves APR, and provide practical guidance for designing LLM-based FL strategies.
title_zh: 基于大语言模型的程序修复中故障定位上下文的作用
abstract_zh: 故障定位（Fault Localization, FL）是基于大语言模型（LLM）的自动化程序修复（APR）中的关键环节，但其实际影响仍缺乏深入研究。具体而言，目前尚不清楚所需的定位精度究竟应达到何种程度，超出预测错误位置的额外上下文是否有益，以及如何有效获取这些上下文。我们针对500个SWE-bench Verified实例，使用GPT-5-mini开展了一项大规模实证研究，评估了61种不同配置，涵盖文件级、元素级和行级上下文的变化。研究结果表明，增加上下文并不总能提升修复性能。文件级定位是决定性因素，在性能上相比“无文件”基线提升了15至17倍。扩展文件上下文通常有助于提升表现，成功修复最常出现在包含约6至10个相关文件的配置中。元素级上下文的扩展仅在文件上下文质量较高的情况下才能带来条件性收益，而行级上下文的扩展则常常因噪声放大而导致性能下降。基于LLM的上下文检索方法总体优于传统的结构启发式方法，且使用的文件数和token更少。总体而言，最有效的故障定位上下文策略通常结合高层抽象层面的广泛语义理解与精确的行级定位。这些发现挑战了“扩大定位上下文必然提升APR效果”的普遍假设，并为设计基于LLM的故障定位策略提供了切实可行的指导。

title: CODESTRUCT: Code Agents over Structured Action Spaces
authors: Myeongsoo Kim, Joe Hsu, Dingmin Wang, Shweta Garg, Varun Kumar, Murali Krishna Ramanathan
abstract: LLM-based code agents treat repositories as unstructured text, applying edits through brittle string matching that frequently fails due to formatting drift or ambiguous patterns. We propose reframing the codebase as a structured action space where agents operate on named AST entities rather than text spans. Our framework, CODESTRUCT, provides readCode for retrieving complete syntactic units and editCode for applying syntax-validated transformations to semantic program elements. Evaluated on SWE-Bench Verified across six LLMs, CODESTRUCT improves Pass@1 accuracy by 1.2-5.0% while reducing token consumption by 12-38% for most models. Models that frequently fail to produce valid patches under text-based interfaces benefit most: GPT-5-nano improves by 20.8% as empty-patch failures drop from 46.6% to 7.2%. On CodeAssistBench, we observe consistent accuracy gains (+0.8-4.4%) with cost reductions up to 33%. Our results show that structure-aware interfaces offer a more reliable foundation for code agents.
title_zh: 代码结构：面向结构化动作空间的代码智能体
abstract_zh: 基于大语言模型的代码代理将代码仓库视为非结构化文本，通过脆弱的字符串匹配方式进行修改，常常因格式变化或模式模糊而失败。我们提出将代码库重新构想为一个结构化的操作空间，使代理在命名的抽象语法树（AST）实体上操作，而非文本片段。我们的框架CODESTRUCT提供了readCode功能，用于检索完整的语法单元，以及editCode功能，可对程序的语义元素执行经过语法验证的转换。在SWE-Bench Verified基准上对六种大语言模型进行评估，CODESTRUCT将Pass@1准确率提升了1.2%至5.0%，同时大多数模型的token消耗降低了12%至38%。在传统文本接口下频繁生成无效补丁的模型受益最为显著：GPT-5-nano的性能提升达20.8%，空补丁失败率从46.6%降至7.2%。在CodeAssistBench上的测试也显示出一致的准确率提升（+0.8%至4.4%），成本降低最高可达33%。结果表明，具备结构感知能力的接口为代码代理提供了更可靠的运作基础。

title: Towards Effective In-context Cross-domain Knowledge Transfer via Domain-invariant-neurons-based Retrieval
authors: Jianzhi Yan, Zhiming Li, Le Liu, Zike Yuan, Shiwei Chen, Youcheng Pan, Buzhou Tang, Yang Xiang, Danny Dongning Sun
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have made notable progress in logical reasoning, yet still fall short of human-level performance. Current boosting strategies rely on expert-crafted in-domain demonstrations, limiting their applicability in expertise-scarce domains, such as specialized mathematical reasoning, formal logic, or legal analysis. In this work, we demonstrate the feasibility of leveraging cross-domain demonstrating examples to boost the LLMs' reasoning performance. Despite substantial domain differences, many reusable implicit logical structures are shared across domains. In order to effectively retrieve cross-domain examples for unseen domains under investigation, in this work, we further propose an effective retrieval method, called domain-invariant neurons-based retrieval (\textbf{DIN-Retrieval}). Concisely, DIN-Retrieval first summarizes a hidden representation that is universal across different domains. Then, during the inference stage, we use the DIN vector to retrieve structurally compatible cross-domain demonstrations for the in-context learning. Experimental results in multiple settings for the transfer of mathematical and logical reasoning demonstrate that our method achieves an average improvement of 1.8 over the state-of-the-art methods \footnote{Our implementation is available at https://github.com/Leon221220/DIN-Retrieval}.
title_zh: 基于域不变神经元的检索实现有效的上下文跨域知识迁移
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在逻辑推理方面已取得显著进展，但仍远未达到人类水平的表现。当前的性能提升策略通常依赖于专家精心设计的领域内示范样本，这限制了其在缺乏专业知识的领域中的应用，例如专门的数学推理、形式逻辑或法律分析等。在本工作中，我们展示了利用跨领域示范样本来提升LLM推理能力的可行性。尽管不同领域间存在显著差异，但许多可复用的隐式逻辑结构在多个领域中是共通的。为了在面对未知领域时有效检索出跨领域的示范样本，本文进一步提出了一种高效的检索方法，称为基于领域不变神经元的检索（\textbf{DIN-Retrieval}）。简而言之，DIN-Retrieval首先提取一个在不同领域间具有普遍性的隐藏表征。在推理阶段，我们使用该DIN向量来检索在结构上与目标问题相匹配的跨领域示范样本，用于上下文学习。在多个数学与逻辑推理迁移任务中的实验结果表明，我们的方法相较于现有最先进方法平均提升了1.8分 \footnote{我们的实现代码可在 https://github.com/Leon221220/DIN-Retrieval 获取}。

title: LLM4CodeRE: Generative AI for Code Decompilation Analysis and Reverse Engineering
authors: Hamed Jelodar, Samita Bai, Tochukwu Emmanuel Nwankwo, Parisa Hamedi, Mohammad Meymani, Roozbeh Razavi-Far, Ali A. Ghorbani
abstract: Code decompilation analysis is a fundamental yet challenging task in malware reverse engineering, particularly due to the pervasive use of sophisticated obfuscation techniques. Although recent large language models (LLMs) have shown promise in translating low-level representations into high-level source code, most existing approaches rely on generic code pretraining and lack adaptation to malicious software. We propose LLM4CodeRE, a domain-adaptive LLM framework for bidirectional code reverse engineering that supports both assembly-to-source decompilation and source-to-assembly translation within a unified model. To enable effective task adaptation, we introduce two complementary fine-tuning strategies: (i) a Multi-Adapter approach for task-specific syntactic and semantic alignment, and (ii) a Seq2Seq Unified approach using task-conditioned prefixes to enforce end-to-end generation constraints. Experimental results demonstrate that LLM4CodeRE outperforms existing decompilation tools and general-purpose code models, achieving robust bidirectional generalization.
title_zh: LLM4CodeRE：用于代码反汇编分析与逆向工程的生成式人工智能
abstract_zh: 代码反编译分析是恶意软件逆向工程中的一个基础但极具挑战性的任务，尤其由于复杂混淆技术的广泛使用。尽管近期的大规模语言模型（LLMs）在将低级代码表示翻译为高级源代码方面展现出潜力，但大多数现有方法依赖于通用代码预训练，缺乏对恶意软件领域的针对性适应。我们提出了 LLM4CodeRE，一种面向双向代码逆向工程的领域自适应大语言模型框架，能够在统一模型中支持从汇编到源码的反编译以及从源码到汇编的转换。为实现有效的任务适配，我们引入了两种互补的微调策略：(i) 多适配器（Multi-Adapter）方法，用于特定任务的语法与语义对齐；(ii) 基于任务条件前缀的序列到序列统一（Seq2Seq Unified）方法，以强制端到端生成约束。实验结果表明，LLM4CodeRE 在性能上超越了现有的反编译工具和通用代码模型，实现了稳健的双向泛化能力。

title: Scientific Graphics Program Synthesis via Dual Self-Consistency Reinforcement Learning
authors: Juekai Lin, Yun Zhu, Honglin Lin, Sijing Li, Tianwei Lin, Zheng Liu, Xiaoyang Wang, Wenqiao Zhang, Lijun Wu
abstract: Graphics Program Synthesis is pivotal for interpreting and editing visual data, effectively facilitating the reverse-engineering of static visuals into editable TikZ code. While TikZ is the de facto standard for scientific schematics due to its programmatic flexibility, its requirement for rigorous spatial precision presents a significant challenge for Multimodal Large Language Models. Progress is currently stifled by two primary gaps: (1) Data Quality Gap: existing image-TikZ corpora often lack strict executability and reliable visual alignment; (2) Evaluation Gap: a lack of benchmarks for both structural and visual fidelity. To address these, we present a closed-loop framework featuring: SciTikZ-230K, a large-scale, high-quality dataset from our Execution-Centric Data Engine covering 11 diverse scientific disciplines; SciTikZ-Bench, a multifaceted benchmark spanning from basic geometric constructs to intricate hierarchical schematics to evaluate both visual fidelity and structural logic. To further broaden the scope of visual-code optimization methodology, we introduce a novel Dual Self-Consistency Reinforcement Learning optimization paradigm, which utilizes Round-Trip Verification to penalize degenerate code and boost overall self-consistency. Empowered by these, our trained model SciTikZer-8B achieves state-of-the-art performance, consistently outperforming proprietary giants like Gemini-2.5-Pro and massive models like Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Instruct.
title_zh: 基于双重自洽强化学习的科学绘图程序合成
abstract_zh: 图形程序合成对于解析和编辑视觉数据至关重要，能够有效实现将静态图像逆向工程为可编辑的TikZ代码。尽管TikZ因其程序化灵活性已成为科学示意图的行业标准，但其对严格空间精度的要求，给多模态大语言模型带来了显著挑战。当前进展受到两大核心瓶颈的制约：（1）数据质量差距：现有图像-TikZ语料库往往缺乏严格的可执行性与可靠的视觉对齐；（2）评估体系缺失：尚无针对结构准确性和视觉保真度的基准评测体系。为解决上述问题，我们提出一个闭环框架，包含：SciTikZ-230K——由我们基于执行优先的数据引擎构建的大规模高质量数据集，涵盖11个不同的科学领域；SciTikZ-Bench——一个多层次的综合性评估基准，从基础几何构造到复杂的分层示意图，全面评估模型在视觉保真度与结构逻辑方面的表现。为进一步拓展视觉-代码优化方法的适用范围，我们引入一种新颖的双自一致性强化学习优化范式，通过往返验证机制惩罚退化代码，显著提升整体自一致性。依托这些创新，我们训练出的模型SciTikZer-8B在性能上达到当前最优水平，持续超越如Gemini-2.5-Pro等商业巨头，以及Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Instruct等超大规模模型。

title: The Model Agreed, But Didn't Learn: Diagnosing Surface Compliance in Large Language Models
authors: Xiaojie Gu, Ziying Huang, Weicong Hong, Jian Xie, Renze Lou, Kai Zhang
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) internalize vast world knowledge as parametric memory, yet inevitably inherit the staleness and errors of their source corpora. Consequently, ensuring the reliability and malleability of these internal representations is imperative for trustworthy real-world deployment. Knowledge editing offers a pivotal paradigm for surgically modifying memory without retraining. However, while recent editors demonstrate high success rates on standard benchmarks, it remains questionable whether current evaluation frameworks that rely on assessing output under specific prompting conditions can reliably authenticate genuine memory modification. In this work, we introduce a simple diagnostic framework that subjects models to discriminative self-assessment under in-context learning (ICL) settings that better reflect real-world application environments, specifically designed to scrutinize the subtle behavioral nuances induced by memory modifications. This probing reveals a pervasive phenomenon of Surface Compliance, where editors achieve high benchmark scores by merely mimicking target outputs without structurally overwriting internal beliefs. Moreover, we find that recursive modifications accumulate representational residues, triggering cognitive instability and permanently diminishing the reversibility of the model's memory state. These insights underscore the risks of current editing paradigms and highlight the pivotal role of robust memory modification in building trustworthy, long-term sustainable LLM systems. Code is available at https://github.com/XiaojieGu/SA-MCQ.
title_zh: 模型同意了，却未学习：诊断大型语言模型中的表面顺从问题
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）将海量世界知识内化为参数化记忆，但不可避免地继承了其语料库中的过时性与错误。因此，确保这些内部表征的可靠性与可塑性，对于实现可信的现实世界部署至关重要。知识编辑提供了一种关键范式，可在不重新训练的前提下对模型记忆进行精准修改。然而，尽管近期的编辑方法在标准基准测试中表现出较高的成功率，目前依赖特定提示条件评估输出的评价框架是否能真正验证内存修改的真实性仍存疑虑。本文提出一种简洁的诊断框架，在上下文学习（ICL）环境下对模型进行判别性自我评估，该环境更贴近真实应用场景，专门用于检测由内存修改引发的细微行为变化。这一探测揭示出一种普遍存在的“表面顺从”现象：编辑器通过简单模仿目标输出获得高分，却未从根本上改写模型的内部信念结构。此外，我们发现递归修改会累积表征残留，引发认知不稳定性，并永久削弱模型记忆状态的可逆性。这些发现凸显了当前编辑范式的潜在风险，强调了构建可靠、长期可持续的LLM系统中稳健记忆修改的关键作用。代码已公开于 https://github.com/XiaojieGu/SA-MCQ。

title: Pre-Execution Safety Gate & Task Safety Contracts for LLM-Controlled Robot Systems
authors: Ike Obi, Vishnunandan L. N. Venkatesh, Weizheng Wang, Ruiqi Wang, Dayoon Suh, Temitope I. Amosa, Wonse Jo, Byung-Cheol Min
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used to convert task commands into robot-executable code, however this pipeline lacks validation gates to detect unsafe and defective commands before they are translated into robot code. Furthermore, even commands that appear safe at the outset can produce unsafe state transitions during execution in the absence of continuous constraint monitoring. In this research, we introduce SafeGate, a neurosymbolic safety architecture that prevents unsafe natural language task commands from reaching robot execution. Drawing from ISO 13482 safety standard, SafeGate extracts structured safety-relevant properties from natural language commands and applies a deterministic decision gate to authorize or reject execution. In addition, we introduce Task Safety Contracts, which decomposes commands that pass through the gate into invariants, guards, and abort conditions to prevent unsafe state transitions during execution. We further incorporate Z3 SMT solving to enforce constraint checking derived from the Task Safety Contracts. We evaluate SafeGate against existing LLM-based robot safety frameworks and baseline LLMs across 230 benchmark tasks, 30 AI2-THOR simulation scenarios, and real-world robot experiments. Results show that SafeGate significantly reduces the acceptance of defective commands while maintaining a high acceptance of benign tasks, demonstrating the importance of pre-execution safety gates for LLM-controlled robot systems
title_zh: 大语言模型控制机器人系统的执行前安全门与任务安全合约
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）正被越来越多地用于将任务指令转换为机器人可执行的代码，然而这一流程缺乏验证机制，在将指令转化为机器人代码之前无法检测出不安全或有缺陷的命令。此外，即使某些指令在初始阶段看似安全，若在执行过程中缺乏持续的约束监控，仍可能导致不安全的状态转移。在本研究中，我们提出了SafeGate——一种神经符号安全架构，旨在阻止不安全的自然语言任务指令进入机器人执行阶段。基于ISO 13482安全标准，SafeGate从自然语言指令中提取结构化的安全相关属性，并通过一个确定性的决策门来决定是否授权执行。此外，我们引入了“任务安全契约”（Task Safety Contracts），将通过安全门的指令分解为不变量、前置条件和中止条件，以防止执行过程中的不安全状态转移。我们进一步结合Z3 SMT求解器，对由任务安全契约推导出的约束进行强制校验。我们在230个基准任务、30个AI2-THOR仿真场景以及真实机器人实验中，将SafeGate与现有的基于LLM的机器人安全框架及基线LLM进行了对比评估。结果表明，SafeGate显著降低了对缺陷指令的接受率，同时保持了对良性任务的高接受率，充分证明了在LLM控制的机器人系统中，执行前安全门的重要性。

title: JTON: A Token-Efficient JSON Superset with Zen Grid Tabular Encoding for Large Language Models
authors: Gowthamkumar Nandakishore
abstract: When LLMs process structured data, the serialization format directly affects cost and context utilization. Standard JSON wastes tokens repeating key names in every row of a tabular array--overhead that scales linearly with row count. This paper presents JTON (JSON Tabular Object Notation), a strict JSON superset whose main idea, Zen Grid, factors column headers into a single row and encodes values with semicolons, preserving JSON's type system while cutting redundancy. Across seven real-world domains, Zen Grid reduces token counts by 15-60% versus JSON compact (28.5% average; 32% with bare_strings). Comprehension tests on 10 LLMs show a net +0.3 pp accuracy gain over JSON: four models improve, three hold steady, and three dip slightly. Generation tests on 12 LLMs yield 100% syntactic validity in both few-shot and zero-shot settings. A Rust/PyO3 reference implementation adds SIMD-accelerated parsing at 1.4x the speed of Python's json module. Code, a 683-vector test suite, and all experimental data are publicly available.
title_zh: JTON：一种面向大语言模型的高效标记JSON超集，采用Zen Grid表格编码方式
abstract_zh: 当大语言模型（LLM）处理结构化数据时，序列化格式直接影响成本与上下文利用率。标准 JSON 在表格数组的每一行中重复键名，造成大量 token 浪费——这种开销随行数线性增长。本文提出 JTON（JSON 表格对象表示法），一种严格的 JSON 超集，其核心思想为“Zen Grid”：将列标题提取至单一行中，并使用分号分隔值，既保留了 JSON 的类型系统，又有效消除了冗余。在七个真实世界领域中的实验表明，Zen Grid 相较于紧凑版 JSON 可减少 15%–60% 的 token 数量（平均降幅 28.5%，启用 bare_strings 时达 32%）。对 10 个 LLM 进行的理解测试显示，相比 JSON，Zen Grid 实现了净 +0.3 个百分点的准确率提升：其中四个模型表现更好，三个保持稳定，三个略有下降。生成测试在 12 个 LLM 上进行，无论少样本还是零样本设置下，均实现 100% 的语法正确性。基于 Rust/PyO3 的参考实现支持 SIMD 加速解析，速度达到 Python 原生 json 模块的 1.4 倍。代码、包含 683 个向量的测试套件以及所有实验数据均已公开可获取。

title: Deep Researcher Agent: An Autonomous Framework for 24/7 Deep Learning Experimentation with Zero-Cost Monitoring
authors: Xiangyue Zhang
abstract: We present \textbf{Deep Researcher Agent}, an open-source framework that enables large language model (LLM) agents to autonomously conduct deep learning experiments around the clock. Unlike existing AI research assistants that focus on paper writing or code generation, our system addresses the full experiment lifecycle: hypothesis formation, code implementation, training execution, result analysis, and iterative refinement. The framework introduces three key innovations: (1) \textbf{Zero-Cost Monitoring} -- a monitoring paradigm that incurs zero LLM API costs during model training by relying solely on process-level checks and log file reads; (2) \textbf{Two-Tier Constant-Size Memory} -- a memory architecture capped at $\sim$5K characters regardless of runtime duration, preventing the unbounded context growth that plagues long-running agents; and (3) \textbf{Minimal-Toolset Leader-Worker Architecture} -- a multi-agent design where each worker agent is equipped with only 3--5 tools, reducing per-call token overhead by up to 73\%. In sustained deployments spanning 30+ days, the framework autonomously completed 500+ experiment cycles across four concurrent research projects, achieving a 52\% improvement over baseline metrics in one project through 200+ automated experiments -- all at an average LLM cost of \$0.08 per 24-hour cycle. Code is available at https://github.com/Xiangyue-Zhang/auto-deep-researcher-24x7.
title_zh: 深度研究者代理：一种支持全天候深度学习实验的自主框架，具备零成本监控功能
abstract_zh: 我们提出**Deep Researcher Agent**，一个开源框架，使大型语言模型（LLM）代理能够全天候自主开展深度学习实验。与现有专注于论文撰写或代码生成的AI研究助手不同，我们的系统覆盖了完整的实验生命周期：假设构建、代码实现、训练执行、结果分析以及迭代优化。该框架引入了三项关键创新：（1）**零成本监控**——一种监控范式，在模型训练期间不产生任何LLM API费用，仅依赖进程级检查和日志文件读取；（2）**两级固定大小内存**——一种内存架构，无论运行时长如何，始终控制在约5K字符以内，有效防止长期运行代理中上下文无限增长的问题；（3）**极简工具集领导者-工作者架构**——一种多代理设计，每个工作代理仅配备3至5个工具，将每次调用的token开销降低高达73%。在持续部署超过30天的场景下，该框架在四个并行研究项目中自主完成了500多个实验周期，其中一项项目通过200多次自动化实验，实现了基准指标52%的提升，且平均每个24小时周期的LLM成本仅为0.08美元。代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/Xiangyue-Zhang/auto-deep-researcher-24x7。

title: Vision-Guided Iterative Refinement for Frontend Code Generation
authors: Hannah Sansford, Derek H. C. Law, Wei Liu, Abhishek Tripathi, Niresh Agarwal, Gerrit J. J. van den Burg
abstract: Code generation with large language models often relies on multi-stage human-in-the-loop refinement, which is effective but very costly - particularly in domains such as frontend web development where the solution quality depends on rendered visual output. We present a fully automated critic-in-the-loop framework in which a vision-language model serves as a visual critic that provides structured feedback on rendered webpages to guide iterative refinement of generated code. Across real-world user requests from the WebDev Arena dataset, this approach yields consistent improvements in solution quality, achieving up to 17.8% increase in performance over three refinement cycles. Next, we investigate parameter-efficient fine-tuning using LoRA to understand whether the improvements provided by the critic can be internalized by the code-generating LLM. Fine-tuning achieves 25% of the gains from the best critic-in-the-loop solution without a significant increase in token counts. Our findings indicate that automated, VLM-based critique of frontend code generation leads to significantly higher quality solutions than can be achieved through a single LLM inference pass, and highlight the importance of iterative refinement for the complex visual outputs associated with web development.
title_zh: 基于视觉引导的迭代优化前端代码生成
abstract_zh: 基于大型语言模型的代码生成通常依赖于多阶段的人工介入式优化，虽然有效但成本高昂——尤其在前端Web开发领域，解决方案的质量取决于渲染后的视觉输出。本文提出一种完全自动化的“批判者在环”（critic-in-the-loop）框架，其中视觉-语言模型（VLM）充当视觉评判者，对生成的网页进行结构化反馈，以指导代码的迭代优化。在来自WebDev Arena数据集的真实用户请求上，该方法实现了持续的性能提升，在三个优化周期内最高可使表现提升17.8%。随后，我们研究了使用LoRA进行参数高效微调，以探究批判者提供的改进是否能够被代码生成大模型内化。实验表明，微调方法在不显著增加token数量的前提下，达到了最佳“批判者在环”方案25%的性能增益。研究结果表明，通过自动化、基于VLM的前端代码生成批判机制，可显著提升解决方案质量，远超单次大模型推理所能达到的效果，并凸显了针对复杂视觉输出的Web开发任务中，迭代优化的重要性。

title: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning with Augmented Step-Level Transitions for LLM Agents
authors: Shuai Zhen, Yanhua Yu, Ruopei Guo, Nan Cheng, Yang Deng
abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents have demonstrated strong capabilities in complex interactive decision-making tasks. However, existing LLM agents typically rely on increasingly long interaction histories, resulting in high computational cost and limited scalability. In this paper, we propose STEP-HRL, a hierarchical reinforcement learning (HRL) framework that enables step-level learning by conditioning only on single-step transitions rather than full interaction histories. STEP-HRL structures tasks hierarchically, using completed subtasks to represent global progress of overall task. By introducing a local progress module, it also iteratively and selectively summarizes interaction history within each subtask to produce a compact summary of local progress. Together, these components yield augmented step-level transitions for both high-level and low-level policies. Experimental results on ScienceWorld and ALFWorld benchmarks consistently demonstrate that STEP-HRL substantially outperforms baselines in terms of performance and generalization while reducing token usage. Our code is available at https://github.com/TonyStark042/STEP-HRL.
title_zh: 用于大语言模型代理的带有增强步骤级转移的分层强化学习
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）代理在复杂的交互式决策任务中展现了强大的能力。然而，现有的LLM代理通常依赖于不断增长的交互历史记录，导致计算成本高昂且可扩展性受限。本文提出了一种分层强化学习（HRL）框架——STEP-HRL，该框架通过仅基于单步转移信息进行学习，实现了步骤级的学习能力，而非依赖完整的交互历史。STEP-HRL以层次化结构组织任务，利用已完成的子任务来表示整体任务的全局进展。通过引入局部进展模块，它能够迭代且有选择性地对每个子任务内的交互历史进行总结，生成紧凑的局部进展摘要。这两项机制共同为高层和低层策略提供了增强的步骤级转移信息。在ScienceWorld和ALFWorld基准测试上的实验结果一致表明，STEP-HRL在性能和泛化能力方面显著优于基线方法，同时大幅降低了token使用量。我们的代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/TonyStark042/STEP-HRL。

title: CAKE: Cloud Architecture Knowledge Evaluation of Large Language Models
authors: Tim Lukas Adam, Phongsakon Mark Konrad, Riccardo Terrenzi, Florian Girardo Lukas, Rahime Yilmaz, Krzysztof Sierszecki, Serkan Ayvaz
abstract: In today's software architecture, large language models (LLMs) serve as software architecture co-pilots. However, no benchmark currently exists to evaluate large language models' actual understanding of cloud-native software architecture. For this reason we present a benchmark called CAKE, which consists of 188 expert-validated questions covering four cognitive levels of Bloom's revised taxonomy -- recall, analyze, design, and implement -- and five cloud-native topics. Evaluation is conducted on 22 model configurations (0.5B--70B parameters) across four LLM families, using three-run majority voting for multiple-choice questions (MCQs) and LLM-as-a-judge scoring for free-responses (FR). Based on this evaluation, four notable findings were identified. First, MCQ accuracy plateaus above 3B parameters, with the best model reaching 99.2\%. Second, free-response scores scale steadily across all cognitive levels. Third, the two formats capture different facets of knowledge, as the MCQ accuracy approaches a ceiling while free-responses continue to differentiate models. Finally, reasoning augmentation (+think) improves free-response quality, while tool augmentation (+tool) degrades performance for small models. These results suggest that the evaluation format fundamentally shapes how we measure architectural knowledge in LLMs.
title_zh: CAKE：大语言模型的云架构知识评估
abstract_zh:
在当今的软件架构中，大型语言模型（LLMs）正扮演着软件架构协作者的角色。然而，目前尚缺乏一个基准来评估大型语言模型对云原生软件架构的实际理解能力。为此，我们提出了一个名为CAKE的基准测试，该基准包含188道由专家验证的问题，覆盖了布卢姆修订版认知分类法中的四个认知层次——回忆、分析、设计和实现，以及五个云原生主题。我们在四个大型语言模型家族的22种模型配置（参数量从0.5B到70B不等）上进行了评估，针对多选题（MCQs）采用三轮多数投票机制，针对开放回答题（FR）则使用“大模型作为评判者”的评分方式。

基于此次评估，我们发现了四个显著结论：第一，多选题的准确率在参数量超过30亿后趋于饱和，表现最佳的模型达到了99.2%的准确率；第二，开放回答题的得分在所有认知层次上均呈现稳定增长趋势；第三，两种评估形式揭示了知识的不同侧面：多选题的准确率已接近上限，而开放回答题仍能有效区分不同模型的能力；第四，引入推理增强（+think）可提升开放回答的质量，而工具增强（+tool）则会降低小模型的表现。

这些结果表明，评估形式从根本上影响了我们衡量大型语言模型在软件架构知识方面能力的方式。

title: An End-to-End Approach for Fixing Concurrency Bugs via SHB-Based Context Extractor
authors: Zhuang Li, Qiuping Yi, Keyang Xiao, Zongcheng Ji, Hongliang Liang
abstract: With the rise of multi-core processors and distributed systems, concurrent programming has become essential yet challenging, primarily due to the non-deterministic nature of thread execution. Manually addressing concurrency bugs is time-consuming and error-prone. Automated Program Repair techniques provide a promising solution. However, developing an end-to-end concurrency bug repair tool is particularly challenging. Most existing tools rely on the assumption that bug-related information is readily available or that concurrency bug contexts are ideally extracted, which is often impractical in real-world scenarios. This paper introduces ConFixAgent, an LLM-driven agent capable of fixing various types of concurrency bugs in an end-to-end manner, eliminating the need for any prior bug-related information. Specifically, we propose a novel context extraction approach designed for concurrency bug repair, utilizing Static Happens-Before Graphs to identify bug-relevant sections.We implemented ConFixAgent and evaluated it across multiple benchmark sets. Our extensive experiments demonstrate that ConFixAgent significantly outperforms state-of-the-art tools in addressing diverse types of concurrency bugs, with its context extraction method markedly enhancing the accuracy of LLM-generated repair solutions.
title_zh: 基于SHB的上下文提取器的端到端并发缺陷修复方法
abstract_zh: 随着多核处理器和分布式系统的兴起，并发编程变得至关重要，但也极具挑战性，主要原因是线程执行具有非确定性。手动修复并发缺陷既耗时又容易出错。自动化程序修复技术为此提供了一种有前景的解决方案。然而，开发一个端到端的并发缺陷修复工具尤为困难。大多数现有工具依赖于缺陷相关信息可轻易获取或并发缺陷上下文能够理想提取的假设，这在实际场景中往往不切实际。本文提出 ConFixAgent，一种基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能代理，能够以端到端的方式修复多种类型的并发缺陷，且无需任何预先的缺陷相关知识。具体而言，我们提出了一种专为并发缺陷修复设计的新颖上下文提取方法，利用静态发生前关系图（Static Happens-Before Graphs）来识别与缺陷相关的代码段。我们实现了 ConFixAgent，并在多个基准测试集上进行了评估。大量实验表明，ConFixAgent 在应对各类并发缺陷方面显著优于当前最先进的工具，其上下文提取方法显著提升了 LLM 生成修复方案的准确性。

title: Understanding: reframing automation and assurance
authors: Robin Bloomfield
abstract: Safety and assurance cases risk becoming detached from the understanding needed for responsible engineering and governance decisions. More broadly, the production and evaluation of critical socio-technical systems increasingly face an understanding challenge: pressures for increased tempo, reduced scrutiny, software complexity, and growing use of AI generated artefacts may produce outputs that appear coherent without supporting genuine human comprehension. We argue that understanding should become an explicit, assessable, and defensible component of decision making: what developers, assessors, and decision makers grasp about system behavior, evidence, assumptions, risks, and residual uncertainty. Drawing on Catherine Elgin's epistemology of understanding, we outline a conceptual foundation and then use Assurance 2.0 as an engineering route to operationalize using structured argumentation, evidence, confidence, defeaters, and theory based automation. This leads to two linked artefacts: an Understanding Basis, which justifies why available understanding is sufficient for a decision, and a Personal Understanding Statement, through which participants make their grasp explicit and challengeable. We also identify risks that automation may improve artefact production while weakening understanding, and we propose initial directions for evaluating both efficacy and epistemic impact.
title_zh: 理解：重新定义自动化与保障
abstract_zh: 安全与保证案例可能脱离负责任工程和治理决策所需的理解基础。更广泛地说，关键社会技术系统的生成与评估正面临日益严峻的理解挑战：加快节奏、减少审查、软件复杂性增加以及人工智能生成成果的广泛应用，可能导致输出看似连贯，却缺乏真实的人类理解支撑。我们认为，理解应成为决策中一个明确、可评估且可辩护的组成部分——即开发者、评估者和决策者对系统行为、证据、假设、风险及残余不确定性的掌握程度。基于凯瑟琳·埃尔金（Catherine Elgin）的理解认识论，我们构建了一个概念基础，并以“保证2.0”（Assurance 2.0）作为工程实现路径，通过结构化论证、证据、置信度、反例（defeaters）以及基于理论的自动化手段加以具体化。这最终形成两个相互关联的成果：一是“理解基础”（Understanding Basis），用以说明现有理解足以支持某项决策的理由；二是“个人理解声明”（Personal Understanding Statement），使参与者能够明确表达自身认知，并接受质疑与检验。此外，我们还识别出自动化可能在提升成果产出效率的同时削弱人类理解的风险，并提出了初步方向，用于评估此类方法在实际效果与认识论影响方面的双重表现。

title: LLM Reasoning as Trajectories: Step-Specific Representation Geometry and Correctness Signals
authors: Lihao Sun, Hang Dong, Bo Qiao, Qingwei Lin, Dongmei Zhang, Saravan Rajmohan
abstract: This work characterizes large language models' chain-of-thought generation as a structured trajectory through representation space. We show that mathematical reasoning traverses functionally ordered, step-specific subspaces that become increasingly separable with layer depth. This structure already exists in base models, while reasoning training primarily accelerates convergence toward termination-related subspaces rather than introducing new representational organization. While early reasoning steps follow similar trajectories, correct and incorrect solutions diverge systematically at late stages. This late-stage divergence enables mid-reasoning prediction of final-answer correctness with ROC-AUC up to 0.87. Furthermore, we introduce trajectory-based steering, an inference-time intervention framework that enables reasoning correction and length control based on derived ideal trajectories. Together, these results establish reasoning trajectories as a geometric lens for interpreting, predicting, and controlling LLM reasoning behavior.
title_zh: 大语言模型推理作为轨迹：步骤特定的表征几何与正确性信号
abstract_zh: 这项工作将大语言模型的思维链生成描述为在表示空间中的一条结构化轨迹。我们发现，数学推理过程会经过功能有序、步骤特定的子空间，且随着网络层数加深，这些子空间逐渐变得更加可分。这种结构在基础模型中已存在，而推理训练主要作用是加速模型向终止相关子空间的收敛，而非引入新的表征组织方式。尽管早期推理步骤的轨迹相似，但正确与错误解法在后期阶段会系统性地分化。这种后期分化使得我们能够在推理过程中中期预测最终答案的正确性，其ROC-AUC最高可达0.87。此外，我们提出了一种基于轨迹的引导方法（trajectory-based steering），这是一种推理时的干预框架，能够根据推导出的理想轨迹实现推理纠错和长度控制。综上所述，这些结果确立了推理轨迹作为理解、预测和控制大语言模型推理行为的一种几何视角。

title: Same Graph, Different Likelihoods: Calibration of Autoregressive Graph Generators via Permutation-Equivalent Encodings
authors: Laurits Fredsgaard, Aaron Thomas, Michael Riis Andersen, Mikkel N. Schmidt, Mahito Sugiyama
abstract: Autoregressive graph generators define likelihoods via a sequential construction process, but these likelihoods are only meaningful if they are consistent across all linearizations of the same graph. Segmented Eulerian Neighborhood Trails (SENT), a recent linearization method, converts graphs into sequences that can be perfectly decoded and efficiently processed by language models, but admit multiple equivalent linearizations of the same graph. We quantify violations in assigned negative log-likelihood (NLL) using the coefficient of variation across equivalent linearizations, which we call Linearization Uncertainty (LU). Training transformers under four linearization strategies on two datasets, we show that biased orderings achieve lower NLL on their native order but exhibit expected calibration error (ECE) two orders of magnitude higher under random permutation, indicating that these models have learned their training linearization rather than the underlying graph. On the molecular graph benchmark QM9, NLL for generated graphs is negatively correlated with molecular stability (AUC $=0.43$), while LU achieves AUC $=0.85$, suggesting that permutation-based evaluation provides a more reliable quality check for generated molecules. Code is available at https://github.com/lauritsf/linearization-uncertainty
title_zh: 相同图，不同似然：通过置换等价编码校准自回归图生成器
abstract_zh: 自回归图生成器通过顺序构建过程定义似然，但这些似然仅在所有同图的不同线性化结果中保持一致时才具有意义。最近提出的分段欧拉邻域路径（SENT）是一种线性化方法，可将图转换为序列，这些序列能够被语言模型完美解码并高效处理，但同一图可能对应多个等价的线性化形式。我们使用等价线性化之间的变异系数来量化所分配负对数似然（NLL）的偏差，这一度量被称为线性化不确定性（LU）。我们在两个数据集上，采用四种不同的线性化策略训练Transformer模型，结果表明：依赖特定顺序的模型在其原生线性化下虽能获得较低的NLL，但在随机排列下的预期校准误差（ECE）却高出两个数量级，说明这些模型学习的是训练时的特定线性化方式，而非图本身的内在结构。在分子图基准数据集QM9上，生成图的NLL与分子稳定性呈负相关（AUC = 0.43），而LU的AUC达到0.85，表明基于排列的评估方式能更可靠地检验生成分子的质量。代码已公开于 https://github.com/lauritsf/linearization-uncertainty

title: Semantic Communication with an LLM-enabled Knowledge Base
authors: Wuxia Hu, Caili Guo, Yang Yang, Chunyan Feng, Kuiyuan Ding, Shiwen Mao
abstract: Semantic communication (SC) can achieve superior coding and transmission performance based on the knowledge contained in the semantic knowledge base (KB). However, conventional KBs consist of source KBs and channel KBs, which are often costly to obtain data and limited in data scale. Fortunately, large language models (LLMs) have recently emerged with extensive knowledge and generative capabilities. Therefore, this paper proposes an SC system with LLM-enabled knowledge base (SC-LMKB), which utilizes the generation ability of LLMs to significantly enrich the KB of SC systems. In particular, we first design an LLM-enabled generation mechanism with a prompt engineering strategy for source data generation (SDG) and a cross-attention alignment method for channel data generation (CDG). However, hallucinations from LLMs may cause semantic noise, thus degrading SC performance. To mitigate the hallucination issue, a cross-domain fusion codec (CDFC) framework with a hallucination filtering phase and a cross-domain fusion phase is then proposed for SDG. In particular, the first phase filters out new data generated by the LMKB irrelevant to the original data based on semantic similarity. Then, a cross-domain fusion phase is proposed, which fuses source data with LLM-generated data based on their semantic importance, thereby enhancing task performance. Besides, a joint training objective that combines cross-entropy loss and reconstruction loss is proposed to reduce the impact of hallucination on CDG. Experiment results on three cross-modality retrieval tasks demonstrate that the proposed SC-LMKB can achieve up to 72.6\% and 90.7\% performance gains compared to conventional SC systems and LLM-enabled SC systems, respectively.
title_zh: 基于大语言模型的知识库语义通信
abstract_zh: 语义通信（SC）能够基于语义知识库（KB）中包含的知识，实现更优的编码与传输性能。然而，传统的知识库由源知识库和信道知识库组成，其数据获取成本高且数据规模有限。幸运的是，大语言模型（LLMs）近年来迅速发展，具备广泛的知识储备和强大的生成能力。因此，本文提出一种基于大语言模型赋能的知识库的语义通信系统（SC-LMKB），利用LLMs的生成能力显著丰富语义通信系统的知识库。具体而言，我们首先设计了一种基于提示工程策略的LLM赋能生成机制，用于源数据生成（SDG），以及一种基于交叉注意力对齐的方法用于信道数据生成（CDG）。然而，LLMs产生的幻觉可能导致语义噪声，从而降低SC性能。为缓解这一问题，我们进一步提出一种跨域融合编解码框架（CDFC），该框架包含幻觉过滤阶段和跨域融合阶段，专门应用于SDG。在第一阶段，通过语义相似度筛选出LMKB生成的与原始数据无关的新数据并予以剔除；随后，提出跨域融合阶段，根据数据的语义重要性将源数据与LLM生成的数据进行融合，从而提升任务性能。此外，针对CDG，我们还设计了一种联合训练目标，结合交叉熵损失与重构损失，以减轻幻觉带来的负面影响。在三个跨模态检索任务上的实验结果表明，所提出的SC-LMKB相较于传统SC系统和基于LLM的SC系统，分别实现了最高达72.6%和90.7%的性能提升。

title: Auditable Agents
authors: Yi Nian, Aojie Yuan, Haiyue Zhang, Jiate Li, Yue Zhao
abstract: LLM agents call tools, query databases, delegate tasks, and trigger external side effects. Once an agent system can act in the world, the question is no longer only whether harmful actions can be prevented--it is whether those actions remain answerable after deployment. We distinguish accountability (the ability to determine compliance and assign responsibility), auditability (the system property that makes accountability possible), and auditing (the process of reconstructing behavior from trustworthy evidence). Our claim is direct: no agent system can be accountable without auditability. To make this operational, we define five dimensions of agent auditability, i.e., action recoverability, lifecycle coverage, policy checkability, responsibility attribution, and evidence integrity, and identify three mechanism classes (detect, enforce, recover) whose temporal information-and-intervention constraints explain why, in practice, no single approach suffices. We support the position with layered evidence rather than a single benchmark: lower-bound ecosystem measurements suggest that even basic security prerequisites for auditability are widely unmet (617 security findings across six prominent open-source projects); runtime feasibility results show that pre-execution mediation with tamper-evident records adds only 8.3 ms median overhead; and controlled recovery experiments show that responsibility-relevant information can be partially recovered even when conventional logs are missing. We propose an Auditability Card for agent systems and identify six open research problems organized by mechanism class.
title_zh: 可审计代理
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）代理能够调用工具、查询数据库、委派任务并触发外部副作用。一旦代理系统具备了在现实世界中行动的能力，问题就不再仅仅是能否防止有害行为——更关键的是，这些行为在部署后是否仍可追溯问责。我们区分了三个核心概念：问责性（即确定合规性并分配责任的能力）、可审计性（使问责成为可能的系统属性），以及审计（从可信证据中重建行为的过程）。我们的主张明确而直接：任何代理系统若缺乏可审计性，就无法实现问责。为使这一主张具有可操作性，我们定义了代理可审计性的五个维度：动作可恢复性、生命周期覆盖性、策略可验证性、责任归属性和证据完整性，并识别出三类机制（检测、强制、恢复），其时间上的信息与干预约束解释了为何在实践中，单一方法无法满足全部需求。我们通过多层次的证据支持该观点，而非依赖单一基准测试：生态下限测量表明，即使是最基本的可审计性安全前提也普遍未达标（在六个知名开源项目中发现617项安全缺陷）；运行时可行性结果表明，采用防篡改记录进行事前中介仅带来8.3毫秒的中位延迟；受控恢复实验则显示，即便传统日志缺失，仍可部分恢复与责任相关的信息。最后，我们提出为代理系统设计“可审计性卡片”，并按机制类别梳理出六个开放的研究问题。

title: Beyond Accuracy: Unveiling Inefficiency Patterns in Tool-Integrated Reasoning
authors: Qisheng Su, Shiting Huang, Zhen Fang, Ziyan Chen, Zehui Chen, Feng Zhao
abstract: In real-world Tool-Integrated Reasoning (TIR) scenarios, where LLMs interleave reasoning with external tool calls, a major source of inefficiency is that the toolcalls create pauses between LLM requests and cause KV-Cache eviction, forcing recomputation. Also, the long, unfiltered response returned by external tools inflates the KV-Cache, so each decode step spends more time loading the growing cache and thus becomes steadily slower as context length increases. However, existing efficiency metrics like token counts and toolcall counts fail to capture the real model inference latency. To address this, we introduce PTE (Prefill Token Equivalents), a hardware-aware TIR-efficiency metric that unifies internal reasoning and external tool-use costs while explicitly accounting for non-reusable KV-Cache and long-tool-response scenarios. Validation in a high-concurrency industrial setting indicates that PTE aligns significantly better with wall-clock latency than standard token counts, while maintaining consistent efficiency rankings across diverse hardware profiles. We conduct extensive experiments across five TIR benchmarks, quantify their PTE costs, and identify four inefficiency patterns that appear in TIR. We also discover that trajectories with higher PTE costs tend to have lower reasoning correctness, indicating that simply using more tools does not improve the quality of the answer.
title_zh: 超越准确性：揭示工具集成推理中的低效模式
abstract_zh:
在现实世界的工具集成推理（Tool-Integrated Reasoning, TIR）场景中，大型语言模型（LLMs）会交替进行内部推理与外部工具调用。这一过程中的主要效率瓶颈在于：工具调用导致LLM请求之间出现延迟，并引发KV缓存（KV-Cache）的逐出，从而迫使模型重新计算。此外，外部工具返回的长而未经过滤的响应会显著膨胀KV缓存，使得每个解码步骤都需要花费更多时间来加载不断增长的缓存，因此随着上下文长度的增加，解码速度持续下降。

然而，现有的效率度量指标（如token数量和工具调用次数）无法准确反映实际的模型推理延迟。为解决这一问题，我们提出了PTE（Prefill Token Equivalents）——一种面向硬件的TIR效率度量标准。PTE将内部推理与外部工具使用成本统一起来，同时显式考虑不可重用的KV缓存以及长工具响应等典型场景的影响。

在高并发工业环境中的验证表明，PTE相较于传统的token计数，在预测实际运行时延（wall-clock latency）方面具有显著更高的相关性，且在不同硬件配置下仍能保持一致的效率排序。我们在五个TIR基准测试上进行了广泛实验，量化了各任务的PTE成本，并识别出四种在TIR中普遍存在的效率低下模式。此外，我们还发现PTE成本较高的推理轨迹往往伴随着更低的推理正确率，这表明单纯增加工具调用次数并不能提升答案质量。

title: PROMISE: Proof Automation as Structural Imitation of Human Reasoning
authors: Youngjoo Ahn, Sangyeop Yeo, Gijung Lim, Jongmin Lee, Jinyoung Yeo, Jieung Kim
abstract: Automated proof generation for formal software verification remains largely unresolved despite advances in large language models (LLMs). While LLMs perform well in NLP, vision, and code generation, formal verification still requires substantial human effort. Interactive theorem proving (ITP) demands manual proof construction under strict logical constraints, limiting scalability; for example, verifying the seL4 microkernel required decades of effort. Existing LLM-based approaches attempt to automate this process but remain limited. Most rely on single-shot generation or shallow retrieval, which may work for small proofs but fail to scale to large, interdependent verification tasks with deep structural dependencies. We present PROMISE (PROof MIning via Structural Embeddings), a structure-aware framework that reframes proof generation as a stateful search over proof-state transitions. Instead of surface-level retrieval, PROMISE mines structural patterns from proof states and tactic transitions, enabling retrieval and adaptation of compatible proof fragments during iterative search. We evaluate PROMISE on the seL4 benchmark across multiple LLM backends and compare it with prior systems such as Selene and Rango. PROMISE consistently outperforms prior methods, achieving up to +26 point improvements (186% relative gain) while maintaining robustness across models, demonstrating the effectiveness of structure-aware proof mining for scalable theorem proving.
title_zh: 承诺：作为人类推理结构模仿的证明自动化
abstract_zh:
尽管大型语言模型（LLMs）取得了进展，自动化形式化软件验证中的证明生成问题仍未得到根本解决。虽然LLMs在自然语言处理、计算机视觉和代码生成方面表现优异，但形式化验证仍需大量人工投入。交互式定理证明（ITP）要求在严格的逻辑约束下手动构建证明，其可扩展性受限；例如，验证seL4微内核就耗费了数十年的努力。现有的基于LLM的方法试图自动化这一过程，但仍存在局限。大多数方法依赖于单次生成或浅层检索，这些方法在小型证明中可能有效，但在具有深层结构依赖关系的大型、相互关联的验证任务中难以扩展。

我们提出PROMISE（PROof MIning via Structural Embeddings），一种注重结构感知的框架，将证明生成重新定义为对证明状态转移的状态化搜索过程。与仅依赖表面信息的检索不同，PROMISE从证明状态和策略转换中挖掘结构模式，从而在迭代搜索过程中实现对兼容证明片段的检索与适配。我们在seL4基准测试上评估了PROMISE，使用多种LLM后端，并与先前系统如Selene和Rango进行对比。实验结果表明，PROMISE始终优于现有方法，在多个模型上实现了最高达+26分的提升（相对增长186%），同时保持了跨模型的鲁棒性，充分证明了结构感知的证明挖掘在实现可扩展定理证明方面的有效性。

title: An Ultra-Low-Power Synthesizable Asynchronous AER Encoder for Neuromorphic Edge Devices
authors: Yihui Wang, Sheng-Yu Peng, Sahil Shah
abstract: This paper presents a fully synthesizable, treebased Address-Event Representation (AER) encoder designed for scalable neuromorphic computing systems. To achieve high throughput while maintaining strict compatibility with commercial EDA workflows, the asynchronous design employs a bundled-data protocol within a semi-decoupled micropipeline. The architecture replaces traditional transparent latches with standard edge-triggered flip-flops, enabling digital synthesis and place-and-route (PnR) using Cadence toolkits. A cross-coupled NAND-based random-priority arbiter is embedded within the encoder of each tree node to resolve event collisions efficiently. An 8-event AER prototype is fabricated in 65 nm CMOS technology utilizing a purely digital standard-cell flow. Post-fabrication silicon measurements validate the design, demonstrating a peak throughput of 33 MEvent/s and an average event latency of 50 ns, equating to a propagation delay of 17 ns/(event-bit). The design consumes only 435 fJ per encoded event.
title_zh: 用于类脑边缘设备的超低功耗可综合异步AER编码器
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种完全可综合的基于树结构的地址-事件表示（AER）编码器，专为可扩展的类脑计算系统设计。为在保持与商用EDA流程严格兼容的同时实现高吞吐量，该异步设计采用半解耦微流水线中的捆绑数据协议。架构中用标准的边沿触发寄存器取代传统的透明锁存器，从而支持使用Cadence工具套件进行数字综合与布局布线（PnR）。每个树节点的编码器中嵌入了一个基于交叉耦合NAND门的随机优先级仲裁器，以高效解决事件冲突问题。该8事件AER原型采用纯数字标准单元流程，在65 nm CMOS工艺下完成流片。流片后硅片测试结果验证了该设计的有效性，实测峰值吞吐量达33 MEvent/s，平均事件延迟为50 ns，相当于17 ns/(event-bit)的传播延迟。该设计每编码一个事件仅消耗435 fJ能量。

title: SignalClaw: LLM-Guided Evolutionary Synthesis of Interpretable Traffic Signal Control Skills
authors: Da Lei, Feng Xiao, Lu Li, Yuzhan Liu
abstract: Traffic signal control TSC requires strategies that are both effective and interpretable for deployment, yet reinforcement learning produces opaque neural policies while program synthesis depends on restrictive domain-specific languages. We present SIGNALCLAW, a framework that uses large language models LLMs as evolutionary skill generators to synthesize and refine interpretable control skills for adaptive TSC. Each skill includes rationale, selection guidance, and executable code, making policies human-inspectable and self-documenting. At each generation, evolution signals from simulation metrics such as queue percentiles, delay trends, and stagnation are translated into natural language feedback to guide improvement. SignalClaw also introduces event-driven compositional evolution: an event detector identifies emergency vehicles, transit priority, incidents, and congestion via TraCI, and a priority dispatcher selects specialized skills. Each skill is evolved independently, and a priority chain enables runtime composition without retraining. We evaluate SignalClaw on routine and event-injected SUMO scenarios against four baselines. On routine scenarios, it achieves average delay of 7.8 to 9.2 seconds, within 3 to 10 percent of the best method, with low variance across random seeds. Under event scenarios, it yields the lowest emergency delay 11.2 to 18.5 seconds versus 42.3 to 72.3 for MaxPressure and 78.5 to 95.3 for DQN, and the lowest transit person delay 9.8 to 11.5 seconds versus 38.7 to 45.2 for MaxPressure. In mixed events, the dispatcher composes skills effectively while maintaining stable overall delay. The evolved skills progress from simple linear rules to conditional strategies with multi-feature interactions, while remaining fully interpretable and directly modifiable by traffic engineers.
title_zh: SignalClaw：基于大模型引导的可解释交通信号控制技能进化合成
abstract_zh: 交通信号控制（TSC）需要既高效又可解释的策略以实现部署，然而强化学习生成的是难以理解的神经网络策略，而程序合成则依赖于受限的领域特定语言。我们提出了SIGNALCLAW框架，该框架利用大型语言模型（LLMs）作为进化技能生成器，用于合成与优化可解释的自适应TSC控制技能。每个技能均包含推理依据、选择指导和可执行代码，使策略具备人类可审查性和自我文档化特性。在每一代进化中，仿真指标（如排队百分位、延误趋势和停滞状态）所生成的进化信号被转化为自然语言反馈，用以指导策略改进。SIGNALCLAW还引入了事件驱动的组合式进化机制：通过TraCI检测紧急车辆、公交优先、事故及拥堵等事件，由优先级调度器选择相应的专用技能。各技能独立演化，通过优先级链实现在运行时的动态组合，无需重新训练。我们在常规场景和注入事件的SUMO场景下对SIGNALCLAW进行了评估，对比了四种基线方法。在常规场景中，其平均延误为7.8至9.2秒，仅比最优方法高出3%至10%，且在不同随机种子下的方差极低。在事件场景下，SIGNALCLAW表现出最低的紧急车辆延误（11.2至18.5秒），显著优于MaxPressure（42.3至72.3秒）和DQN（78.5至95.3秒），同时在公交乘客延误方面也达到最低水平（9.8至11.5秒），远低于MaxPressure的38.7至45.2秒。在混合事件情况下，调度器能有效组合各类技能，同时保持整体延误稳定。所演化的技能从简单的线性规则逐步发展为包含多特征交互的条件策略，同时始终保持完全可解释性，并可由交通工程师直接修改和调整。

title: Target Policy Optimization
authors: Jean Kaddour
abstract: In RL, given a prompt, we sample a group of completions from a model and score them. Two questions follow: which completions should gain probability mass, and how should the parameters move to realize that change? Standard policy-gradient methods answer both at once, so the update can overshoot or undershoot depending on the learning rate, clipping, and other optimizer choices. We introduce \emph{Target Policy Optimization} (TPO), which separates the two questions. Given scored completions, TPO constructs a target distribution $q_i \propto p_i^{\,\mathrm{old}} \exp(u_i)$ and fits the policy to it by cross-entropy. The loss gradient on sampled-completion logits is $p^θ- q$, which vanishes once the policy matches the target. On tabular bandits, transformer sequence tasks, and billion-parameter LLM RLVR, TPO matches PG, PPO, GRPO, and DG on easy tasks and substantially outperforms them under sparse reward. Code is available at https://github.com/JeanKaddour/tpo.
title_zh: 目标策略优化
abstract_zh: 在强化学习中，给定一个提示（prompt），我们从模型中采样一组补全结果并对其进行评分。接下来有两个问题：哪些补全结果应获得概率质量？以及参数应如何调整以实现这一变化？标准的策略梯度方法同时回答这两个问题，因此更新过程可能因学习率、裁剪策略及其他优化器选择而出现过冲或欠冲。我们提出了**目标策略优化**（Target Policy Optimization, TPO），将这两个问题分离处理。在得到评分后的补全结果后，TPO 构造一个目标分布 $ q_i \propto p_i^{\,\mathrm{old}} \exp(u_i) $，并通过交叉熵将策略拟合到该目标分布。在采样补全结果的 logits 上，损失函数的梯度为 $ p^θ - q $，当策略与目标分布完全匹配时，该梯度将消失。在表格型老虎机、Transformer 序列任务以及百亿参数大模型的 RLVR 任务中，TPO 在简单任务上表现与 PG、PPO、GRPO 和 DG 相当，而在稀疏奖励场景下则显著优于这些方法。代码已开源，地址为 https://github.com/JeanKaddour/tpo。

title: A Formal Security Framework for MCP-Based AI Agents: Threat Taxonomy, Verification Models, and Defense Mechanisms
authors: Nirajan Acharya, Gaurav Kumar Gupta
abstract: The Model Context Protocol (MCP), introduced by Anthropic in November 2024 and now governed by the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation, has rapidly become the de facto standard for connecting large language model (LLM)-based agents to external tools and data sources, with over 97 million monthly SDK downloads and more than 177000 registered tools. However, this explosive adoption has exposed a critical gap: the absence of a unified, formal security framework capable of systematically characterizing, analyzing, and mitigating the diverse threats facing MCP-based agent ecosystems. Existing security research remains fragmented across individual attack papers, isolated benchmarks, and point defense mechanisms. This paper presents MCPSHIELD, a comprehensive formal security framework for MCP-based AI agents. We make four principal contributions: (1) a hierarchical threat taxonomy comprising 7 threat categories and 23 distinct attack vectors organized across four attack surfaces, grounded in the analysis of over 177000 MCP tools; (2) a formal verification model based on labeled transition systems with trust boundary annotations that enables static and runtime analysis of MCP tool interaction chains; (3) a systematic comparative evaluation of 12 existing defense mechanisms, identifying coverage gaps across our threat taxonomy; and (4) a defense in depth reference architecture integrating capability based access control, cryptographic tool attestation, information flow tracking, and runtime policy enforcement. Our analysis reveals that no existing single defense covers more than 34 percent of the identified threat landscape, whereas MCPSHIELD's integrated architecture achieves theoretical coverage of 91 percent. We further identify seven open research challenges that must be addressed to secure the next generation of agentic AI systems.
title_zh: 基于MCP的AI代理的形式化安全框架：威胁分类、验证模型与防御机制
abstract_zh:
模型上下文协议（MCP）由Anthropic于2024年11月首次提出，现由Linux基金会旗下的智能体人工智能基金会（Agentic AI Foundation）负责管理，已迅速成为连接基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能体与外部工具及数据源的事实标准。目前，MCP的SDK每月下载量已超过9700万次，注册工具数量突破17.7万个。然而，其爆炸式增长暴露出一个关键短板：缺乏一个统一、正式的安全框架，无法系统性地识别、分析和缓解MCP生态中面临的多样化威胁。当前的安全研究仍分散于单个攻击论文、孤立的基准测试以及局部防御机制之间，难以形成整体防护能力。

本文提出了MCPSHIELD——一个面向MCP驱动AI智能体的综合性形式化安全框架。我们做出四项核心贡献：  
（1）构建了一个分层威胁分类体系，涵盖7类威胁和23种具体攻击向量，按四个攻击面进行组织，该体系基于对超过17.7万个MCP工具的深入分析；  
（2）设计了一种基于带信任边界标注的标记转移系统的形式化验证模型，支持对MCP工具交互链的静态与运行时分析；  
（3）对12种现有防御机制进行了系统性对比评估，揭示了这些方案在我们威胁分类体系中的覆盖盲区；  
（4）提出一种纵深防御参考架构，集成基于能力的访问控制、加密工具认证、信息流追踪以及运行时策略强制等多重机制。

我们的分析表明，任何单一现有防御措施所覆盖的威胁范围均不超过34%，而MCPSHIELD的整合架构在理论上可实现高达91%的威胁覆盖率。此外，本文还识别出七个亟待解决的关键开放研究挑战，以保障下一代智能体AI系统的安全性。

title: QiMeng-PRepair: Precise Code Repair via Edit-Aware Reward Optimization
authors: Changxin Ke, Rui Zhang, Jiaming Guo, Yuanbo Wen, Li Ding, Shuo Wang, Xuyuan Zhu, Xiong Peng, Di Huang, Zidong Du, Xing Hu, Qi Guo, Yunji Chen
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve strong program repair performance but often suffer from over-editing, where excessive modifications overwrite correct code and hinder bug localization. We systematically quantify its impact and introduce precise repair task, which maximizes reuse of correct code while fixing only buggy parts. Building on this insight, we propose PRepair, a framework that mitigates over-editing and improves repair accuracy. PRepair has two components: Self-Breaking, which generates diverse buggy programs via controlled bug injection and min-max sampling, and Self-Repairing, which trains models with Edit-Aware Group Relative Policy Optimization (EA-GRPO) using an edit-aware reward to encourage minimal yet correct edits.
