Experiments show that PRepair improves repair precision by up to 31.4% under $\mathrm{fix}_1@1$, a metric that jointly considers repair correctness and extent, and significantly increases decoding throughput when combined with speculative editing, demonstrating its potential for precise and practical code repair.
title_zh: Qimeng-PRepair：通过编辑感知奖励优化实现精准代码修复
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在程序修复任务中表现出色，但常常存在过度修改的问题：过多的代码改动会覆盖原本正确的部分，从而干扰错误定位。我们系统地量化了这一问题的影响，并提出了一种更精确的修复任务范式，旨在最大限度地复用正确代码的同时，仅修复有缺陷的部分。基于这一洞察，我们提出了PRepair框架，有效缓解了过度修改问题并提升了修复准确性。PRepair包含两个核心组件：Self-Breaking通过受控注入错误和最小-最大采样生成多样化的错误程序；Self-Repair则采用一种面向编辑的组相对策略优化方法（EA-GRPO），结合面向编辑的奖励机制，引导模型进行最少但正确的修改。实验结果表明，PRepair在$\mathrm{fix}_1@1$指标下将修复精度最高提升了31.4%，该指标综合考虑了修复的正确性与修改范围；同时，在结合推测性编辑时，显著提高了解码吞吐量，充分展现了其在实现精准且实用代码修复方面的巨大潜力。

title: Can Large Language Models Reinvent Foundational Algorithms?
authors: Jian Zhao, Haoren Luo, Yu Wang, Yuhan Cao, Pingyue Sheng, Tianxing He
abstract: LLMs have shown strong potential to advance scientific discovery. Whether they possess the capacity for foundational innovation, however, remains an open question. In this work, we focus on a prerequisite for foundational innovation: can LLMs reinvent foundational algorithms in computer science? Our \textit{Unlearn-and-Reinvent} pipeline applies LLM unlearning to remove a specific foundational algorithm, such as Dijkstra's or Euclid's algorithm, from an LLM's pretrained knowledge, and then tests whether the model can reinvent it in a controlled environment. To enable effective unlearning, we adopt a GRPO-based, on-policy unlearning method. Across 10 target algorithms, 3 strong open-weight models, and 3 hint levels, our experiments demonstrate that (1) the strongest model Qwen3-4B-Thinking-2507 successfully reinvents 50% of the algorithms with no hint, 70% at hint level 1, and 90% at hint level 2; (2) a few high-level hints can enhance the reinvention success rate, but even step-by-step hints fail for those complicated algorithms; and (3) test-time reinforcement learning enables successful reinvention for the Strassen algorithm at hint level 2. Through analyses of output trajectories and ablation studies, we find that generative verifier in the reinvention phase plays a critical role in sustaining models' reasoning strength, helping to avoid the ``thought collapse'' phenomenon. These findings offer insights into both the potential and current limits of LLMs' innovative thinking.
title_zh: 大型语言模型能否重新发明基础算法？
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLMs）在推动科学发现方面展现出巨大潜力。然而，它们是否具备基础性创新的能力，仍是待解之问。本文聚焦于基础性创新的一个先决条件：大语言模型能否重新发明计算机科学中的基础算法？我们提出了一个名为“Unlearn-and-Reinvent”的管道方法，通过让大语言模型“遗忘”特定的基础算法（如Dijkstra算法或欧几里得算法）的预训练知识，再在受控环境中测试其是否能够重新发明这些算法。为实现有效的遗忘，我们采用基于GRPO的、在线策略的遗忘方法。在10个目标算法、3个强大的开源模型以及3种提示水平的实验中，我们的研究结果表明：（1）最强的模型Qwen3-4B-Thinking-2507在无提示条件下成功重发明了50%的算法，在提示等级1下达到70%，在提示等级2下达到90%；（2）少量高层次提示可显著提升重发明成功率，但即使是逐步提示也难以应对复杂算法；（3）在提示等级2下，通过测试时的强化学习机制，模型成功重发明了斯特拉森（Strassen）算法。通过对生成轨迹的分析和消融实验，我们发现重发明阶段的生成式验证器在维持模型推理能力方面起着关键作用，有助于避免“思维崩溃”现象的发生。这些发现为理解大语言模型在创新思维方面的潜力与当前局限提供了重要启示。

title: HYVE: Hybrid Views for LLM Context Engineering over Machine Data
authors: Jian Tan, Fan Bu, Yuqing Gao, Dev Khanolkar, Jason Mackay, Boris Sobolev, Lei Jin, Li Zhang
abstract: Machine data is central to observability and diagnosis in modern computing systems, appearing in logs, metrics, telemetry traces, and configuration snapshots. When provided to large language models (LLMs), this data typically arrives as a mixture of natural language and structured payloads such as JSON or Python/AST literals. Yet LLMs remain brittle on such inputs, particularly when they are long, deeply nested, and dominated by repetitive structure. We present HYVE (HYbrid ViEw), a framework for LLM context engineering for inputs containing large machine-data payloads, inspired by database management principles. HYVE surrounds model invocation with coordinated preprocessing and postprocessing, centered on a request-scoped datastore augmented with schema information. During preprocessing, HYVE detects repetitive structure in raw inputs, materializes it in the datastore, transforms it into hybrid columnar and row-oriented views, and selectively exposes only the most relevant representation to the LLM. During postprocessing, HYVE either returns the model output directly, queries the datastore to recover omitted information, or performs a bounded additional LLM call for SQL-augmented semantic synthesis. We evaluate HYVE on diverse real-world workloads spanning knowledge QA, chart generation, anomaly detection, and multi-step network troubleshooting. Across these benchmarks, HYVE reduces token usage by 50-90% while maintaining or improving output quality. On structured generation tasks, it improves chart-generation accuracy by up to 132% and reduces latency by up to 83%. Overall, HYVE offers a practical approximation to an effectively unbounded context window for prompts dominated by large machine-data payloads.
title_zh: HYVE：面向机器数据的大型语言模型上下文工程混合视图
abstract_zh:
机器数据在现代计算系统的可观测性与诊断中占据核心地位，广泛出现在日志、指标、遥测追踪以及配置快照中。当这些数据被提供给大型语言模型（LLMs）时，通常以自然语言与结构化数据（如JSON或Python/AST字面量）混合的形式呈现。然而，LLM在处理此类输入时仍表现脆弱，尤其当输入较长、嵌套深度大且以重复性结构为主时。我们提出HYVE（HYbrid ViEw）——一种面向包含大规模机器数据负载输入的LLM上下文工程框架，其设计灵感源自数据库管理理念。HYVE在模型调用前后引入协调一致的预处理与后处理流程，核心是一个带有模式信息增强的请求作用域数据存储。

在预处理阶段，HYVE能够检测原始输入中的重复结构，将其在数据存储中物化，并转换为混合列式与行式视图，仅将最相关的一种表示形式传递给LLM。在后处理阶段，HYVE可直接返回模型输出，也可查询数据存储以恢复遗漏信息，或执行一次有限的额外LLM调用，实现基于SQL增强的语义合成。我们在涵盖知识问答、图表生成、异常检测及多步骤网络故障排查等多样化真实工作负载上对HYVE进行了评估。在各项基准测试中，HYVE将token使用量降低了50%-90%，同时保持甚至提升了输出质量。在结构化生成任务中，其将图表生成准确率提升高达132%，延迟降低最多达83%。总体而言，HYVE为那些以大规模机器数据负载为主的提示提供了接近无限上下文窗口的实际解决方案。

title: LLMs Should Express Uncertainty Explicitly
authors: Junyu Guo, Shangding Gu, Ming Jin, Costas Spanos, Javad Lavaei
abstract: Large language models are increasingly used in settings where uncertainty must drive decisions such as abstention, retrieval, and verification. Most existing methods treat uncertainty as a latent quantity to estimate after generation rather than a signal the model is trained to express. We instead study uncertainty as an interface for control. We compare two complementary interfaces: a global interface, where the model verbalizes a calibrated confidence score for its final answer, and a local interface, where the model emits an explicit <uncertain> marker during reasoning when it enters a high-risk state. These interfaces provide different but complementary benefits. Verbalized confidence substantially improves calibration, reduces overconfident errors, and yields the strongest overall Adaptive RAG controller while using retrieval more selectively. Reasoning-time uncertainty signaling makes previously silent failures visible during generation, improves wrong-answer coverage, and provides an effective high-recall retrieval trigger. Our findings further show that the two interfaces work differently internally: verbal confidence mainly refines how existing uncertainty is decoded, whereas reasoning-time signaling induces a broader late-layer reorganization. Together, these results suggest that effective uncertainty in LLMs should be trained as task-matched communication: global confidence for deciding whether to trust a final answer, and local signals for deciding when intervention is needed.
title_zh: 大语言模型应明确表达不确定性
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型在需要以不确定性驱动决策的场景中日益广泛应用，例如选择不回答、检索信息或验证结果。目前大多数方法将不确定性视为生成后需估计的潜在变量，而非模型在训练过程中应表达的信号。本文则将不确定性视为一种控制接口进行研究。我们比较了两种互补的接口：一种是全局接口，即模型为其最终答案提供一个校准过的置信度评分；另一种是局部接口，即当模型在推理过程中进入高风险状态时，会显式发出一个<uncertain>标记。这两种接口虽各有侧重，但能相互补充。口头表达的置信度显著提升了模型的校准能力，减少了过度自信的错误，并在使用检索时更加有选择性，从而实现了最强的自适应RAG控制器。而推理过程中的不确定性信号则使原本无声的失败在生成过程中变得可见，提高了对错误答案的覆盖范围，并作为高效的高召回率检索触发机制。我们的研究还发现，这两种接口在内部运作方式上存在差异：口头置信度主要优化了现有不确定性的解码方式，而推理时的信号则引发了更广泛的后期层重构。综合来看，这些结果表明，大型语言模型中的有效不确定性应当被训练为与任务相匹配的沟通机制：全局置信度用于判断是否信任最终答案，局部信号则用于判断何时需要干预。

title: FLARE: Agentic Coverage-Guided Fuzzing for LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Mingxuan Hui, Xinyue Li, Lu Wang, Chengcheng Wan, Yifan Wang, Yimian Wang, Feiyue Song, Beining Shi, Yixi Li, Yaxiao Li
abstract: Multi-Agent LLM Systems (MAS) have been adopted to automate complex human workflows by breaking down tasks into subtasks. However, due to the non-deterministic behavior of LLM agents and the intricate interactions between agents, MAS applications frequently encounter failures, including infinite loops and failed tool invocations. Traditional software testing techniques are ineffective in detecting such failures due to the lack of LLM agent specification, the large behavioral space of MAS, and semantic-based correctness judgment. This paper presents FLARE, a novel testing framework tailored for MAS. FLARE takes the source code of MAS as input and extracts specifications and behavioral spaces from agent definitions. Based on these specifications, FLARE builds test oracles and conducts coverage-guided fuzzing to expose failures. It then analyzes execution logs to judge whether each test has passed and generates failure reports. Our evaluation on 16 diverse open-source applications demonstrates that FLARE achieves 96.9% inter-agent coverage and 91.1% intra-agent coverage, outperforming baselines by 9.5% and 1.0%. FLARE also uncovers 56 previously unknown failures unique to MAS.
title_zh: FLARE：基于代理覆盖引导的模糊测试方法，用于基于大语言模型的多智能体系统
abstract_zh: 多智能体大语言模型系统（MAS）通过将复杂的人类工作流程分解为子任务，已被广泛用于自动化流程。然而，由于大语言模型智能体的非确定性行为以及智能体之间复杂的交互，MAS应用经常出现故障，包括无限循环和工具调用失败等问题。传统的软件测试技术在检测此类故障时效果不佳，原因在于缺乏对LLM智能体的明确定义、MAS庞大的行为空间，以及基于语义的正确性判断难度较大。本文提出了一种专为MAS设计的新颖测试框架——FLARE。FLARE以MAS的源代码为输入，从智能体定义中提取规范和行为空间；基于这些规范，FLARE构建测试断言并进行覆盖引导的模糊测试，以暴露潜在缺陷；随后分析执行日志，判断每个测试是否通过，并生成故障报告。我们在16个多样化的开源应用上进行了评估，结果表明，FLARE实现了96.9%的跨智能体覆盖率和91.1%的单智能体内覆盖率，分别优于基线方法9.5%和1.0%。此外，FLARE还发现了56个此前未知且仅存在于MAS中的独特故障。

title: gyaradax: Local Gyrokinetics JAX Code
authors: Gianluca Galletti, Eric Volkmann, Johannes Brandstetter
abstract: Gyrokinetic simulations are essential for understanding and controlling turbulence in fusion plasmas, yet they are oftentimes implemented in legacy codebases, in many cases CPU-bound. These are both hard to maintain and especially incompatible with optimization and ML workflows. gyaradax is a minimal JAX/CUDA solver for local flux-tube gyrokinetics. We base our implementation on GKW (Peeters et al., 2009), but with added native GPU acceleration and automatic differentiation. We validate gyaradax against analytical cases and empirical benchmarks, achieving formal agreement and statistical parity with GKW alongside a substantial speedup. We deliberately and extensively utilized agentic workflows in this project. A key contribution is showing that coding agents, guided by human expertise, structured prompting, and measurable progress through unit testing enabled extremely fast translation of complex Fortran code, and further optimizations. Gyaradax facilitates research at the intersection of ML and plasma physics. We showcase this through practical examples in inverse problems and sensitivity analysis.
title_zh: gyaradax：本地磁流体动力学JAX代码
abstract_zh:
gyaradax 是一个用于局部通量管吉罗动力学的轻量级 JAX/CUDA 求解器，旨在解决融合等离子体湍流的模拟与控制问题。尽管吉罗动力学模拟对于理解等离子体行为至关重要，但它们通常依赖于老旧的代码库，且在许多情况下受限于 CPU 性能。这类代码不仅难以维护，而且与优化和机器学习（ML）工作流极不兼容。我们基于 GKW（Peeters 等，2009）的实现，开发了 gyaradax，引入了原生 GPU 加速和自动微分功能。通过与解析解及经验基准进行对比验证，gyaradax 在形式上与 GKW 保持一致，并在统计上达到高度一致性，同时实现了显著的速度提升。

本项目中，我们有意识地、广泛地采用了智能代理（agentic）工作流。一个关键贡献是证明：在人类专家指导、结构化提示设计以及通过单元测试衡量进展的共同作用下，编码代理能够以极快的速度完成复杂 Fortran 代码的迁移，并进一步实现性能优化。gyaradax 为机器学习与等离子体物理交叉领域的研究提供了有力支持。我们通过反问题求解和敏感性分析等实际案例，展示了其应用潜力。

title: From Hallucination to Structure Snowballing: The Alignment Tax of Constrained Decoding in LLM Reflection
authors: Hongxu Zhou
abstract: Intrinsic self-correction in Large Language Models (LLMs) frequently fails in open-ended reasoning tasks due to ``hallucination snowballing,'' a phenomenon in which models recursively justify early errors during free-text reflection. While structured feedback can mitigate this issue, existing approaches often rely on externally trained critics or symbolic tools, reducing agent autonomy. This study investigates whether enforcing structured reflection purely through Outlines-based constrained decoding can disrupt error propagation without additional training. Evaluating an 8-billion-parameter model (Qwen3-8B), we show that simply imposing structural constraints does not improve self-correction performance. Instead, it triggers a new failure mode termed ``structure snowballing.'' We find that the cognitive load required to satisfy strict formatting rules pushes the model into formatting traps. This observation helps explain why the agent achieves near-perfect superficial syntactic alignment yet fails to detect or resolve deeper semantic errors. These findings expose an ``alignment tax'' inherent to constrained decoding, highlighting a tension between structural granularity and internal model capacity in autonomous workflows. Code and raw logs are available in the GitHub repository: https://github.com/hongxuzhou/agentic_llm_structured_self_critique.
title_zh: 从幻觉到结构雪崩：大语言模型反思中约束解码的对齐代价
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）中的内在自我修正在开放式推理任务中经常失效，原因在于“幻觉雪球效应”——即模型在自由文本反思过程中反复为早期错误提供看似合理的解释。尽管结构化反馈能够缓解这一问题，但现有方法通常依赖外部训练的评判器或符号工具，从而削弱了智能体的自主性。本研究探讨：仅通过基于Outlines的约束解码强制实施结构化反思，是否能够在不额外训练的情况下打破错误传播。我们对一个80亿参数的模型（Qwen3-8B）进行了评估，结果表明，单纯施加结构约束并不能提升自我修正性能；相反，它引发了一种新的失败模式，称为“结构雪球效应”。我们发现，严格格式要求带来的认知负荷迫使模型陷入格式陷阱。这一观察有助于解释为何该智能体在表面语法层面几乎达到完美对齐，却无法识别或解决深层语义错误。这些发现揭示了约束解码固有的“对齐代价”，凸显了在自主工作流中，结构精细度与模型内部容量之间的张力。代码和原始日志已发布于GitHub仓库：https://github.com/hongxuzhou/agentic_llm_structured_self_critique。

title: Arch: An AI-Native Hardware Description Language for Register-Transfer Clocked Hardware Design
authors: Shuqing Zhao
abstract: We present Arch (AI-native Register-transfer Clocked Hardware), a hardware description language designed from first principles for micro-architecture specification and AI-assisted code generation. Arch introduces first-class language constructs for pipelines, FSMs, FIFOs, arbiters, register files, buses, and clock-domain crossings -- structures that existing HDLs express only as user-defined patterns prone to subtle errors. A central design choice is that clocks and resets are themselves parameterized types (Clock<D>, Reset<S,P,D?>) rather than ordinary nets, converting clock-domain crossing (CDC) and reset-domain crossing (RDC) analysis from external linter passes into compile-time typing rules. Combined with simultaneous tracking of bit widths, port directions, single-driver ownership, and combinational acyclicity, the type system catches multiple drivers, undriven ports, implicit latches, width mismatches, combinational loops, and unsynchronized domain crossings before any simulator runs. Every syntactic choice is governed by an AI-generatability contract: an LL(1) grammar requiring no backtracking or multi-token lookahead, no preprocessor or macros, a uniform declaration schema, named block endings, explicit directional connect arrows, and a todo! escape hatch enable LLMs to produce structurally correct, type-safe Arch from natural-language specifications without fine-tuning. The Arch compiler emits deterministic, lint-clean IEEE 1800-2017 SystemVerilog and provides an integrated simulation toolchain that generates compiled C++ models for cycle-accurate simulation. We present case studies of an 8-way set-associative L1 data cache and a synthesizable PG021-compatible AXI DMA controller (with Yosys and OpenSTA results on Sky130), and compare Arch to SystemVerilog, VHDL, Chisel, Bluespec, and other modern HDLs across expressiveness, safety, and AI suitability dimensions.
title_zh: Arch：一种面向寄存器传输时钟硬件设计的AI原生硬件描述语言
abstract_zh:
我们提出 Arch（AI 原生寄存器传输时钟硬件），一种从零开始设计的硬件描述语言，专为微架构规范和 AI 辅助代码生成而打造。Arch 引入了对流水线、有限状态机（FSM）、FIFO、仲裁器、寄存器文件、总线以及跨时钟域（CDC）等结构的一等语言构造——这些在现有 HDL 中仅能通过用户自定义模式表达，极易引入细微错误。其核心设计理念是：时钟与复位本身作为参数化类型（Clock<D>、Reset<S,P,D?>），而非普通信号网表，从而将跨时钟域（CDC）和跨复位域（RDC）分析从外部 lint 检查阶段转变为编译时的类型规则。

结合对位宽、端口方向、单驱动所有权以及组合逻辑无环性的同步追踪，Arch 的类型系统可在任何仿真运行前捕获多重驱动、未驱动端口、隐式锁存器、位宽不匹配、组合环路及未同步的域间跨越等问题。每一处语法设计均遵循 AI 可生成性契约：采用无需回溯或多标记前瞻的 LL(1) 语法，不依赖预处理器或宏，具有统一的声明模式，使用命名块结束符，明确的方向连接箭头，以及一个名为 todo! 的逃生机制，使得大语言模型（LLM）能够在无需微调的情况下，仅凭自然语言规格说明生成结构正确、类型安全的 Arch 代码。

Arch 编译器可输出确定性、无 lint 错误的 IEEE 1800-2017 SystemVerilog 代码，并提供集成的仿真工具链，能够生成可编译的 C++ 模型，实现周期精确仿真。我们展示了两个案例研究：一个八路组相联的 L1 数据缓存，以及一个可综合的 PG021 兼容 AXI DMA 控制器（基于 Yosys 和 OpenSTA 在 Sky130 工艺上的综合与时序分析结果）。此外，我们将 Arch 与 SystemVerilog、VHDL、Chisel、Bluespec 等现代 HDL 在表达能力、安全性与 AI 适配性等多个维度进行了对比分析。

title: Evolutionary Optimization of AI-Collapsed Software Development Stacks: Labor Tipping Points and Workforce Realignment
authors: Matthew H. Kilbane
abstract: This paper presents a quantitative framework for optimizing human AI workforce allocation in software development, translatable to other labor categories. I formalize baseline and AI-collapsed labor models, derive tipping point equations for safe headcount reduction, and embed them in a multi objective evolutionary optimization setup. NSGAII experiments reveal reproducible, phase specific automation strategies that reduce cost while maintaining quality and stable workloads.
title_zh: 人工智能压缩软件开发栈的演化优化：劳动力临界点与人才再配置
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种定量框架，用于优化软件开发中的人机劳动力配置，该框架可推广至其他劳动类别。我们建立了基准模型与AI压缩后的劳动力模型，推导出安全裁员人数的临界点方程，并将其嵌入多目标进化优化系统中。通过NSGA-II算法的实验结果表明，可获得可重复、具有阶段特性的自动化策略，在保持质量与稳定工作量的同时降低人力成本。

title: From Large Language Model Predicates to Logic Tensor Networks: Neurosymbolic Offer Validation in Regulated Procurement
authors: Cedric Haufe, Frieder Stolzenburg
abstract: We present a neurosymbolic approach, i.e., combining symbolic and subsymbolic artificial intelligence, to validating offer documents in regulated public institutions. We employ a language model to extract information and then aggregate with an LTN (Logic Tensor Network) to make an auditable decision. In regulated public institutions, decisions must be made in a manner that is both factually correct and legally verifiable. Our neurosymbolic approach allows existing domain-specific knowledge to be linked to the semantic text understanding of language models. The decisions resulting from our pipeline can be justified by predicate values, rule truth values, and corresponding text passages, which enables rule checking based on a real corpus of offer documents. Our experiments on a real corpus show that the proposed pipeline achieves performance comparable to existing models, while its key advantage lies in its interpretability, modular predicate extraction, and explicit support for XAI (Explainable AI).
title_zh: 从大语言模型谓词到逻辑张量网络：神经符号方法在受监管采购中的可行性验证
abstract_zh: 我们提出一种神经符号方法，即结合符号化与非符号化人工智能，用于监管型公共机构中的投标文件验证。我们采用语言模型提取信息，并通过逻辑张量网络（LTN）进行聚合，从而做出可审计的决策。在受监管的公共机构中，决策必须既符合事实，又具备法律可追溯性。我们的神经符号方法能够将现有的领域知识与语言模型的语义理解能力相结合。由该流程生成的决策可通过谓词取值、规则真值以及相应的文本段落进行解释，从而实现基于真实投标文件语料库的规则核查。我们在真实语料库上的实验表明，所提出的流程性能可与现有模型相媲美，其核心优势在于可解释性、模块化的谓词提取，以及对可解释人工智能（XAI）的明确支持。

title: Gym-Anything: Turn any Software into an Agent Environment
authors: Pranjal Aggarwal, Graham Neubig, Sean Welleck
abstract: Computer-use agents hold the promise of assisting in a wide range of digital economic activities. However, current research has largely focused on short-horizon tasks over a limited set of software with limited economic value, such as basic e-commerce and OS-configuration tasks. A key reason is that creating environments for complex software requires significant time and human effort, and therefore does not scale. To address this, we introduce Gym-Anything, a framework for converting any software into an interactive computer-use environment. We frame environment creation itself as a multi-agent task: a coding agent writes setup scripts, downloads real-world data, and configures the software, while producing evidence of correct setup. An independent audit agent then verifies evidence for the environment setup against a quality checklist. Using a taxonomy of economically valuable occupations grounded in U.S. GDP data, we apply this pipeline to 200 software applications with broad occupational coverage. The result is CUA-World, a collection of over 10K long-horizon tasks spanning domains from medical science and astronomy to engineering and enterprise systems, each configured with realistic data along with train and test splits. CUA-World also includes CUA-World-Long, a challenging long-horizon benchmark with tasks often requiring over 500 steps, far exceeding existing benchmarks. Distilling successful trajectories from the training split into a 2B vision-language model outperforms models 2$\times$ its size. We also apply the same auditing principle at test time: a separate VLM reviews completed trajectories and provides feedback on what remains, improving Gemini-3-Flash on CUA-World-Long from 11.5% to 14.0%. We release all code, infrastructure, and benchmark data to facilitate future research in realistic computer-use agents.
title_zh: Gym-Anything：将任何软件转变为智能体环境
abstract_zh: 计算机使用代理有望在广泛的数字经济活动中提供帮助。然而，当前的研究大多集中于在有限的软件集合上执行短期任务，且这些任务的经济价值较低，例如基础电子商务和操作系统配置等。其主要原因在于：构建复杂软件环境需要大量时间和人力投入，难以实现规模化。为解决这一问题，我们提出了Gym-Anything——一个将任意软件转化为交互式计算机使用环境的框架。我们将环境创建本身视为一项多智能体任务：编程智能体负责编写设置脚本、下载真实世界数据并配置软件，同时生成环境正确配置的证据；独立的审计智能体则根据质量检查清单验证这些证据。基于美国GDP数据所构建的具有经济价值的职业分类体系，我们利用该流程对200个软件应用进行了处理，覆盖广泛的职业领域。由此产生的成果是CUA-World，一个包含超过1万项长期任务的综合性数据集，涵盖医学科学、天文学、工程学以及企业系统等多个领域，每个任务均配备真实数据，并划分了训练集与测试集。CUA-World还包含CUA-World-Long，这是一个极具挑战性的长期任务基准，其中许多任务需完成超过500步操作，远超现有基准水平。我们将训练集中成功轨迹提炼为一个20亿参数的视觉-语言模型（VLM），其性能优于规模为其两倍的其他模型。此外，我们在测试阶段也应用了相同的审计原则：通过一个独立的VLM对已完成的任务轨迹进行审查，并提供改进建议，使Gemini-3-Flash在CUA-World-Long上的表现从11.5%提升至14.0%。我们已公开所有代码、基础设施及基准数据，以推动未来在真实计算机使用代理领域的研究发展。

title: GTaP: A GPU-Resident Fork-Join Task-Parallel Runtime with a Pragma-Based Interface
authors: Yuki Maeda, Kenjiro Taura
abstract: Graphics Processing Units (GPUs) excel at regular data-parallel workloads where massive hardware parallelism can be readily exploited. In contrast, many important irregular applications are naturally expressed as task parallelism with a fork-join control structure. While CPU runtimes for fork-join task parallelism are mature, it remains challenging to efficiently support it on GPUs. We propose GTaP, a GPU-resident runtime that supports fork-join task parallelism. GTaP is based on the persistent kernel model, and supports two worker granularities: thread blocks and individual threads. To realize fork-join on GPUs, GTaP represents joins as continuations and executes each task as a state machine that can be split into multiple execution segments. We also extend Clang's frontend with a pragma-based programming model that enables programmers to express fork-join without exposing low-level mechanisms. GTaP employs work stealing for load balancing, providing better scalability than a global-queue approach. For thread-level workers, we further introduce Execution-Path-Aware Queueing (EPAQ), which allows programmers to partition task queues using user-defined criteria, reducing warp divergence caused by mixing heterogeneous control flows within a warp. Across representative irregular applications, GTaP outperforms OpenMP task-parallel execution on a 72-core CPU in many cases, especially for large problem sizes with compute-intensive tasks. We also show that GTaP's design choices outperform naive GPU alternatives. The benefit of EPAQ is workload-dependent: it can improve performance for some benchmarks while having little effect on others; on Fibonacci, EPAQ achieves up to a 1.8$\times$ speedup.
title_zh: GTaP：一种基于指令的GPU驻留分叉-合并任务并行运行时系统
abstract_zh: 图形处理单元（GPU）在可轻松利用大规模硬件并行性的规则数据并行任务中表现出色。相比之下，许多重要的不规则应用天然适合以分叉-合并控制结构表达的任务并行模式。尽管CPU上的分叉-合并任务并行运行时系统已相当成熟，但在GPU上高效支持这种并行模式仍具挑战性。为此，我们提出了GTaP——一种驻留于GPU的运行时系统，用于支持分叉-合并任务并行。GTaP基于持久内核模型，支持两种工作粒度：线程块和单个线程。为在GPU上实现分叉-合并，GTaP将“合并”操作表示为延续（continuations），并将每个任务执行为一个状态机，该状态机可被划分为多个执行段。此外，我们还扩展了Clang前端，引入了一种基于#pragma指令的编程模型，使程序员能够表达分叉-合并逻辑，而无需暴露底层机制。GTaP采用工作窃取（work stealing）策略进行负载均衡，相比全局队列方法具有更好的可扩展性。对于线程级工作单元，我们进一步提出执行路径感知队列（Execution-Path-Aware Queueing, EPAQ），允许程序员根据自定义标准对任务队列进行分区，从而减少因同一warp内混合异构控制流所导致的warp发散问题。在代表性不规则应用上的实验表明，GTaP在多数情况下性能优于72核CPU上的OpenMP任务并行执行，尤其在大规模、计算密集型任务场景下表现更优。我们还验证了GTaP设计选择相较于简单GPU方案的优势。EPAQ的收益具有依赖性：对某些基准测试能显著提升性能，而对其他则影响较小；在Fibonacci基准上，EPAQ最高可带来1.8倍的加速比。

title: Automating Manual Tasks through Intuitive Robot Programming and Cognitive Robotics
authors: Bijan Kavousian, Petar Tesic, Oliver Petrovic, Christian Brecher
abstract: This paper presents a novel concept for intuitive end-user programming of robots, inspired by natural interaction between humans. Natural language and supportive gestures are translated into robot programs using large language models (LLMs) and computer vision (CV). Through equally natural system feedback in the form of clarification questions and visual representations, the generated program can be reviewed and adjusted, thereby ensuring safety, transparency, and user acceptance.
title_zh: 通过直观的机器人编程与认知机器人技术自动化手动任务
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种新颖的机器人直观用户编程概念，灵感源自人与人之间的自然互动。通过大型语言模型（LLMs）和计算机视觉（CV），将自然语言及辅助手势转化为机器人程序。系统以同样自然的方式提供反馈，如澄清性问题和可视化表示，使生成的程序能够被审查和调整，从而确保安全性、透明性以及用户的接受度。

title: AutoSOTA: An End-to-End Automated Research System for State-of-the-Art AI Model Discovery
authors: Yu Li, Chenyang Shao, Xinyang Liu, Ruotong Zhao, Peijie Liu, Hongyuan Su, Zhibin Chen, Qinglong Yang, Anjie Xu, Yi Fang, Qingbin Zeng, Tianxing Li, Jingbo Xu, Fengli Xu, Yong Li, Tie-Yan Liu
abstract: Artificial intelligence research increasingly depends on prolonged cycles of reproduction, debugging, and iterative refinement to achieve State-Of-The-Art (SOTA) performance, creating a growing need for systems that can accelerate the full pipeline of empirical model optimization. In this work, we introduce AutoSOTA, an end-to-end automated research system that advances the latest SOTA models published in top-tier AI papers to reproducible and empirically improved new SOTA models. We formulate this problem through three tightly coupled stages: resource preparation and goal setting; experiment evaluation; and reflection and ideation. To tackle this problem, AutoSOTA adopts a multi-agent architecture with eight specialized agents that collaboratively ground papers to code and dependencies, initialize and repair execution environments, track long-horizon experiments, generate and schedule optimization ideas, and supervise validity to avoid spurious gains. We evaluate AutoSOTA on recent research papers collected from eight top-tier AI conferences under filters for code availability and execution cost. Across these papers, AutoSOTA achieves strong end-to-end performance in both automated replication and subsequent optimization. Specifically, it successfully discovers 105 new SOTA models that surpass the original reported methods, averaging approximately five hours per paper. Case studies spanning LLM, NLP, computer vision, time series, and optimization further show that the system can move beyond routine hyperparameter tuning to identify architectural innovation, algorithmic redesigns, and workflow-level improvements. These results suggest that end-to-end research automation can serve not only as a performance optimizer, but also as a new form of research infrastructure that reduces repetitive experimental burden and helps redirect human attention toward higher-level scientific creativity.
title_zh: AutoSOTA：一种端到端的自动化研究系统，用于发现最先进的人工智能模型
abstract_zh: 人工智能研究越来越依赖于反复的模型复现、调试与迭代优化，以实现顶尖性能（SOTA），这催生了对能够加速整个实证模型优化流程的系统日益增长的需求。在本工作中，我们提出了AutoSOTA——一个端到端的自动化研究系统，能够将顶级人工智能论文中发表的最新SOTA模型，转化为可复现且在实证上表现更优的新SOTA模型。我们将这一任务建模为三个紧密耦合的阶段：资源准备与目标设定；实验评估；反思与创意生成。为应对该挑战，AutoSOTA采用了一种包含八个专业智能体的多智能体架构，这些智能体协同完成从论文到代码及依赖项的精准映射，初始化并修复执行环境，追踪长期实验过程，生成并调度优化思路，并监督结果有效性，以避免虚假性能提升。我们在来自八场顶级人工智能会议的近期研究论文上评估了AutoSOTA，筛选标准包括代码可获取性和执行成本可控性。在这些论文中，AutoSOTA在自动化复现和后续优化方面均表现出强劲的端到端性能。具体而言，它成功发现了105个超越原始报告方法的新SOTA模型，平均每个论文耗时约五小时。涵盖大语言模型（LLM）、自然语言处理（NLP）、计算机视觉、时间序列分析及优化等多个领域的案例研究进一步表明，该系统不仅能突破常规超参数调优的局限，还能识别出架构创新、算法重构以及工作流层面的改进。这些结果表明，端到端的研究自动化不仅可作为性能优化工具，更可作为一种新型科研基础设施，减轻重复性实验负担，帮助研究人员将注意力转向更高层次的科学创造力。

title: COSMO-Agent: Tool-Augmented Agent for Closed-loop Optimization,Simulation,and Modeling Orchestration
authors: Liyuan Deng, Shujian Deng, Yongkang Chen, Yongkang Dai, Zhihang Zhong, Linyang Li, Xiao Sun, Yilei Shi, Huaxi Huang
abstract: Iterative industrial design-simulation optimization is bottlenecked by the CAD-CAE semantic gap: translating simulation feedback into valid geometric edits under diverse, coupled constraints. To fill this gap, we propose COSMO-Agent (Closed-loop Optimization, Simulation, and Modeling Orchestration), a tool-augmented reinforcement learning (RL) framework that teaches LLMs to complete the closed-loop CAD-CAE process. Specifically, we cast CAD generation, CAE solving, result parsing, and geometry revision as an interactive RL environment, where an LLM learns to orchestrate external tools and revise parametric geometries until constraints are satisfied. To make this learning stable and industrially usable, we design a multi-constraint reward that jointly encourages feasibility, toolchain robustness, and structured output validity. In addition, we contribute an industry-aligned dataset that covers 25 component categories with executable CAD-CAE tasks to support realistic training and evaluation. Experiments show that COSMO-Agent training substantially improves small open-source LLMs for constraint-driven design, exceeding large open-source and strong closed-source models in feasibility, efficiency, and stability.
title_zh: COSMO-Agent：用于闭环优化、仿真与建模编排的工具增强型智能体
abstract_zh: 迭代式工业设计-仿真优化过程受到CAD与CAE之间语义鸿沟的制约：难以在多种耦合约束条件下，将仿真反馈有效转化为合理的几何修改。为弥合这一鸿沟，我们提出COSMO-Agent（闭环优化、仿真与建模编排），一种基于工具增强的强化学习（RL）框架，旨在教会大语言模型（LLM）完成从CAD生成到CAE求解、结果解析再到几何修正的闭环流程。具体而言，我们将CAD生成、CAE求解、结果解析和几何修订过程构建成一个交互式强化学习环境，使LLM学会协调外部工具并逐步调整参数化几何结构，直至满足所有约束条件。为确保该学习过程稳定且具备工业实用性，我们设计了一种多约束奖励机制，同时鼓励可行性、工具链鲁棒性以及输出结构的正确性。此外，我们还构建了一个面向工业应用的数据集，涵盖25类典型零部件，包含可执行的CAD-CAE任务，以支持真实场景下的训练与评估。实验结果表明，COSMO-Agent的训练显著提升了小型开源LLM在约束驱动设计任务中的表现，在可行性、效率和稳定性方面均超越了大型开源模型及性能强劲的闭源模型。

title: LanG -- A Governance-Aware Agentic AI Platform for Unified Security Operations
authors: Anes Abdennebi, Nadjia Kara, Laaziz Lahlou, Hakima Ould-Slimane
abstract: Modern Security Operations Centers struggle with alert fatigue, fragmented tooling, and limited cross-source event correlation. Challenges that current Security Information Event Management and Extended Detection and Response systems only partially address through fragmented tools. This paper presents the LLM-assisted network Governance (LanG), an open-source, governance-aware agentic AI platform for unified security operations contributing: (i) a Unified Incident Context Record with a correlation engine (F1 = 87%), (ii) an Agentic AI Orchestrator on LangGraph with human-in-the-loop checkpoints, (iii) an LLM-based Rule Generator finetuned on four base models producing deployable Snort 2/3, Suricata, and YARA rules (average acceptance rate 96.2%), (iv) a Three-Phase Attack Reconstructor combining Louvain community detection, LLM-driven hypothesis generation, and Bayesian scoring (87.5% kill-chain accuracy), and (v) a layered Governance-MCP-Agentic AI-Security architecture where all tools are exposed via the Model Context Protocol, governed by an AI Governance Policy Engine with a two-layer guardrail pipeline (regex + Llama Prompt Guard 2 semantic classifier, achieving 98.1% F1 score with experimental zero false positives). Designed for Managed Security Service Providers, the platform supports multi-tenant isolation, role-based access, and fully local deployment. Finetuned anomaly and threat detectors achieve weighted F1 scores of 99.0% and 91.0%, respectively, in intrusion-detection benchmarks, running inferences in $\approx$21 ms with a machine-side mean time to detect of 1.58 s, and the rule generator exceeds 91% deployability on live IDS engines. A systematic comparison against eight SOC platforms confirms that LanG uniquely satisfies multiple industrial capabilities all in one open-source tool, while enforcing selected AI governance policies.
title_zh: LanG — 一种面向治理的智能代理AI平台，实现统一安全运营
abstract_zh: 现代安全运营中心（SOC）面临告警疲劳、工具碎片化以及跨源事件关联能力有限等挑战，而当前的安全信息与事件管理（SIEM）及扩展检测与响应（XDR）系统仅通过零散的工具部分缓解这些问题。本文提出了一种基于大语言模型（LLM）辅助的网络治理平台——LanG（LLM-assisted Network Governance），这是一个开源、具备治理意识的智能体式AI平台，旨在实现统一的安全运营。该平台贡献包括：（i）统一事件上下文记录（Unified Incident Context Record）及其相关性引擎，F1得分为87%；（ii）基于LangGraph构建的智能体式AI编排器，支持“人在回路”（human-in-the-loop）检查点；（iii）基于LLM的规则生成器，经过四个基础模型微调后可生成可部署的Snort 2/3、Suricata和YARA规则，平均接受率达96.2%；（iv）三阶段攻击重构器，结合Louvain社区发现算法、LLM驱动的假设生成与贝叶斯评分机制，实现87.5%的杀伤链还原准确率；（v）分层的“治理-MCP-智能体式AI-安全”架构，所有工具均通过模型上下文协议（Model Context Protocol, MCP）暴露，由AI治理策略引擎统一管控，并采用双层护栏管道（正则表达式 + Llama Prompt Guard 2语义分类器），在实验中实现98.1%的F1分数，且零误报（zero false positives）。该平台专为托管安全服务提供商（MSSP）设计，支持多租户隔离、基于角色的访问控制以及完全本地化部署。微调后的异常检测与威胁检测模块在入侵检测基准测试中分别取得99.0%和91.0%的加权F1得分，推理耗时约21毫秒，机器端平均检测时间仅为1.58秒，规则生成器在实际IDS引擎上的部署成功率超过91%。与八款主流SOC平台进行系统性对比表明，LanG是唯一一个在单一开源工具中同时满足多项工业级安全能力，并能有效执行选定AI治理策略的解决方案。

title: TRACE: Capability-Targeted Agentic Training
authors: Hangoo Kang, Tarun Suresh, Jon Saad-Falcon, Azalia Mirhoseini
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) deployed in agentic environments must exercise multiple capabilities across different task instances, where a capability is performing one or more actions in a trajectory that are necessary for successfully solving a subset of tasks in the environment. Many existing approaches either rely on synthetic training data that is not targeted to the model's actual capability deficits in the target environment or train directly on the target environment, where the model needs to implicitly learn the capabilities across tasks. We introduce TRACE (Turning Recurrent Agent failures into Capability-targeted training Environments), an end-to-end system for environment-specific agent self-improvement. TRACE contrasts successful and failed trajectories to automatically identify lacking capabilities, synthesizes a targeted training environment for each that rewards whether the capability was exercised, and trains a LoRA adapter via RL on each synthetic environment, routing to the relevant adapter at inference. Empirically, TRACE generalizes across different environments, improving over the base agent by +14.1 points on $τ^2$-bench (customer service) and +7 perfect scores on ToolSandbox (tool use), outperforming the strongest baseline by +7.4 points and +4 perfect scores, respectively. Given the same number of rollouts, TRACE scales more efficiently than baselines, outperforming GRPO and GEPA by +9.2 and +7.4 points on $τ^2$-bench.
title_zh: 追踪：面向能力目标的代理训练
abstract_zh: 在代理型环境中部署的大语言模型（LLMs）必须在不同任务实例中展现出多种能力，其中“能力”指在轨迹中执行一个或多个动作，这些动作对于成功解决环境中某一子集任务是必要的。现有许多方法要么依赖于非针对性的合成训练数据，这些数据并未针对模型在目标环境中的实际能力短板；要么直接在目标环境中进行训练，导致模型需要隐式地从各类任务中学习所需能力。我们提出了TRACE（将重复代理失败转化为能力导向的训练环境），这是一个面向特定环境的代理自我改进端到端系统。TRACE通过对比成功与失败的轨迹，自动识别出缺失的能力，为每种缺失能力构建一个针对性的合成训练环境，该环境会奖励是否成功执行了相应能力，并基于强化学习（RL）为每个合成环境训练一个LoRA适配器，在推理阶段根据需求路由至相应的适配器。实证结果表明，TRACE在不同环境中均具有良好的泛化能力：在$τ^2$-bench（客户服务）上比基础模型提升14.1分，在ToolSandbox（工具使用）上实现7次完美得分；分别优于最强基线7.4分和4次完美得分。在相同回放次数下，TRACE的扩展效率高于基线方法，在$τ^2$-bench上分别超越GRPO和GEPA 9.2分和7.4分。

title: Spec Kit Agents: Context-Grounded Agentic Workflows
authors: Pardis Taghavi, Santosh Bhavani
abstract: Spec-driven development (SDD) with AI coding agents provides a structured workflow, but agents often remain "context blind" in large, evolving repositories, leading to hallucinated APIs and architectural violations. We present Spec Kit Agents, a multi-agent SDD pipeline (with PM and developer roles) that adds phase-level, context-grounding hooks. Read-only probing hooks ground each stage (Specify, Plan, Tasks, Implement) in repository evidence, while validation hooks check intermediate artifacts against the environment. We evaluate 128 runs covering 32 features across five repositories. Context-grounding hooks improve judged quality by +0.15 on a 1-5 composite LLM-as-judge score (+3.0 percent of the full score; Wilcoxon signed-rank, p < 0.05) while maintaining 99.7-100 percent repository-level test compatibility. We further evaluate the framework on SWE-bench Lite, where augmentation hooks improve baseline by 1.7 percent, achieving 58.2 percent Pass@1.
title_zh: 规范套件代理：基于上下文的代理工作流
abstract_zh: 基于规范的开发（SDD）结合AI编码代理，提供了一种结构化的开发流程。然而，在大型且不断演进的代码库中，这些代理往往存在“上下文盲”问题，导致生成错误的API和违反架构规范的情况。我们提出了Spec Kit Agents，一种多代理SDD流水线（包含项目经理与开发者角色），通过引入阶段级的上下文锚定机制来解决这一问题。只读探测钩子将每个阶段（需求定义、规划、任务分解、实现）都基于代码库中的实际证据进行锚定，而验证钩子则用于检查中间产物是否符合实际环境。我们在五个代码库中对32个功能进行了128次实验评估。结果显示，上下文锚定钩子使LLM作为评判者给出的综合评分（1-5分制）提升了+0.15分（相当于总分提升3.0%；Wilcoxon符号秩检验，p < 0.05），同时保持了99.7%至100%的代码库级测试兼容性。此外，我们在SWE-bench Lite上进一步评估了该框架，发现增强钩子使基线性能提升了1.7%，达到58.2%的Pass@1通过率。

title: CritBench: A Framework for Evaluating Cybersecurity Capabilities of Large Language Models in IEC 61850 Digital Substation Environments
authors: Gustav Keppler, Moritz Gstür, Veit Hagenmeyer
abstract: The advancement of Large Language Models (LLMs) has raised concerns regarding their dual-use potential in cybersecurity. Existing evaluation frameworks overwhelmingly focus on Information Technology (IT) environments, failing to capture the constraints, and specialized protocols of Operational Technology (OT). To address this gap, we introduce CritBench, a novel framework designed to evaluate the cybersecurity capabilities of LLM agents within IEC 61850 Digital Substation environments. We assess five state-of-the-art models, including OpenAI's GPT-5 suite and open-weight models, across a corpus of 81 domain-specific tasks spanning static configuration analysis, network traffic reconnaissance, and live virtual machine interaction. To facilitate industrial protocol interaction, we develop a domain-specific tool scaffold. Our empirical results show that agents reliably execute static structured-file analysis and single-tool network enumeration, but their performance degrades on dynamic tasks. Despite demonstrating explicit, internalized knowledge of the IEC 61850 standards terminology, current models struggle with the persistent sequential reasoning and state tracking required to manipulate live systems without specialized tools. Equipping agents with our domain-specific tool scaffold significantly mitigates this operational bottleneck. Code and evaluation scripts are available at: https://github.com/GKeppler/CritBench
title_zh: CritBench：一种用于评估大语言模型在IEC 61850数字化变电站环境中网络安全能力的框架
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）的快速发展引发了对其在网络安全领域双重用途潜力的担忧。现有的评估框架大多聚焦于信息技术（IT）环境，未能充分反映工业控制系统（OT）环境中的约束条件和专用协议。为弥补这一空白，我们提出了CritBench——一个专为评估LLM代理在IEC 61850数字化变电站环境中网络安全能力而设计的新颖框架。我们在涵盖静态配置分析、网络流量侦察以及实时虚拟机交互等81项领域特定任务的语料库上，对五种前沿模型（包括OpenAI的GPT-5系列及开源权重模型）进行了评估。为支持工业协议的交互，我们开发了一套领域专用的工具框架。实验结果表明，当前模型在静态结构化文件分析和单工具网络枚举任务中表现稳定可靠，但在动态任务上的性能显著下降。尽管这些模型展现出对IEC 61850标准术语的明确且内化的知识，却仍难以胜任操作实时系统所必需的持续性序列推理与状态追踪，除非配备专门工具。通过引入我们开发的领域专用工具框架，显著缓解了这一操作瓶颈。代码与评估脚本可访问：https://github.com/GKeppler/CritBench

title: From Incomplete Architecture to Quantified Risk: Multimodal LLM-Driven Security Assessment for Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Shaofei Huang, Christopher M. Poskitt, Lwin Khin Shar
abstract: Cyber-physical systems often contend with incomplete architectural documentation or outdated information resulting from legacy technologies, knowledge management gaps, and the complexity of integrating diverse subsystems over extended operational lifecycles. This architectural incompleteness impedes reliable security assessment, as inaccurate or missing architectural knowledge limits the identification of system dependencies, attack surfaces, and risk propagation pathways. To address this foundational challenge, this paper introduces ASTRAL (Architecture-Centric Security Threat Risk Assessment using LLMs), an architecture-centric security assessment technique implemented in a prototype tool powered by multimodal LLMs. The proposed approach assists practitioners in reconstructing and analysing CPS architectures when documentation is fragmented or absent. By leveraging prompt chaining, few-shot learning, and architectural reasoning, ASTRAL extracts and synthesises system representations from disparate data sources. By integrating LLM reasoning with architectural modelling, our approach supports adaptive threat identification and quantitative risk estimation for cyber-physical systems. We evaluated the approach through an ablation study across multiple CPS case studies and an expert evaluation involving 14 experienced cybersecurity practitioners. Practitioner feedback suggests that ASTRAL is useful and reliable for supporting architecture-centric security assessment. Overall, the results indicate that the approach can support more informed cyber risk management decisions.
title_zh: 从不完整架构到量化风险：基于多模态大模型的网络物理系统安全评估
abstract_zh: 网络物理系统（CPS）常常面临架构文档不完整或过时的问题，这主要源于遗留技术、知识管理的缺失，以及在长期运行生命周期中整合多种异构子系统所带来的复杂性。这种架构信息的不完整性严重阻碍了可靠的网络安全评估，因为不准确或缺失的架构知识会限制对系统依赖关系、攻击面及风险传播路径的识别。为应对这一基础性挑战，本文提出了一种名为ASTRAL（基于大语言模型的以架构为中心的安全威胁风险评估）的方法，并开发了一个原型工具，该工具由多模态大语言模型驱动。所提出的方案能够在文档碎片化或缺失的情况下，协助实践者重建并分析CPS的架构。通过采用提示链（prompt chaining）、少样本学习（few-shot learning）和架构推理技术，ASTRAL能够从分散的数据源中提取并综合出系统的表示。通过将大语言模型的推理能力与架构建模相结合，本方法支持对网络物理系统进行自适应的威胁识别和定量风险评估。我们通过多个CPS案例研究的消融实验，以及由14名经验丰富的网络安全专家参与的专家评估，对方法进行了验证。实践者的反馈表明，ASTRAL在支持以架构为中心的安全评估方面具有实用性和可靠性。总体而言，结果表明该方法能够为更明智的网络风险决策提供有力支持。
